The Luminit solution uses a layer of holographic diffuser film placed over the luminaire source to manipulate light directionality. Hinduism is the third largest worldreligion. It was founded around 6000 years ago and is the oldest religion in existence. It is followed by 850 million people around the world. These include people in India, Nepal, Mauritius, Fiji, Indonesia, Malaysia and the Caribbean. It is also the main religion of a sizeable part of the populations of Sri Lanka, Bangladesh and Pakistan. A small number are found in other countries.

The Dangers of “Big Data”


Big Data is often used to refer to the process of extracting insights from large sets of digital records. It usually has three components: 1) big sensors to generate a lot of data, 2) big computers to store and process the data, and 3) big people to analyze the data. The result is supposed to be valuable insights for businesses.


The first two components of Big Data are, on the whole, good things. However, as companies increasingly try to harness all of the data that they can, they’re also finding that there can be some negative outcomes. Let’s take a look at some of the potential problems associated with our obsession with Big Data.


1) It’s easy to lie with Big Data

One of the biggest risks of big data is that it’s very easy to lie with it — as with any statistics that one manipulates in order to get the result that one wants.


For instance, one can adjust statistical modeling to produce results that confirm any hypothesis or any predetermined outcome. It’s also easy to use the data in a self-selecting survey to select for respondents who already agree with what you’re saying. It’s even easy to simply make the data up.


This sort of data manipulation

How to find the reasons that are making the battery not charging:

  1. The phone is turned off.  Make sure that the phone is ON.  In some new phones it is possible to turn the phone ON even when the battery is completely discharged.  In this situation check the phone manual that usually comes with the phone.
  2. The phone is in sleep mode.  After a few minutes of inactivity, the phone enters a power saving mode and turns off some functions in order to preserve power.  If you suspect this, then do the following: hold down the power button until the menu appears and select “WAKE UP” or push and hold the power button for a few seconds before checking if the battery still doesn’t charge.
  3. The process of evaluating the accuracy of a statistical model using a holdout dataset is a common practice in machine learning. This procedure helps ensure that any observed improvement in performance when testing on the holdout dataset is attributable to randomness rather than overfitting. However, some argue that this approach may lead to underestimating the generalization ability of a given model. To understand why this might be the case, let's dive deeper into the concept of "model bias," its significance in statistics, and how it relates to the evaluation process using a holdout dataset.

To begin with, let's clarify the distinction between bias and variance in the context of statistical modeling. Often, these two terms are conflated; however, they refer to distinct yet interrelated concepts. Bias refers to the systematic error inherent in a model due to oversimplified assumptions made during modeling, while variance denotes the degree of inconsistency in predictions obtained through multiple repeated modeling exercises using different samples drawn from the same population. Simply put, bias measures the accuracy of a model on average, whereas variance quantifies the reliability of individual prediction outputs regarding their precision.

Model bias emerges from making too few assumptions about underlying patterns within data, while too many assumptions result in high variance. Ideally, minimizing both biases and variances yields superior

This article will explore the history of the lottery, its origin and development, and its current legal status in the United States. What is the difference between a non-compliance and an exception?

1 Answer

The difference between a non-compliance and exception  is:

  • when you are applying an offset that is not allowed under the program, that is a Non-Compliance.

You will get a 111-11A message and will not see any data in the report.

  • when you are applying an offset that is not allowed under the program, but is allowed elsewhere under the program.  that is an Exception.

You will get a 111-11 message and will see data in your report.

Bass fishing is the pursuit of the species of two North American freshwater fish, known as largemouth bass and smallmouth bass, but including various other related species worldwide. Bass fishing is often a competitive sport, with bass fishing tournaments, the largest of which include anglers with professional status, teams representing companies, and celebrities. History.

Why we're still using lead acid batteries

In 2010, one of my colleagues at the Royal Institution of the United Kingdom, Tim Maly, gave an interesting talk which included the observation that one of the most common elements in our everyday lives was lead. “That’s not too surprising,” I remember thinking. After all, lots of lead is used in making car batteries, and that is not surprising because lead makes a good electrical conductor. 

However, not all the uses of lead are obvious. If you are reading this, you are probably in a house, and there is a reasonable chance that your house is constructed from lead. Your house probably has lead paint on the walls, and a lead roof. Lead is used extensively in the glazes of ceramic pottery for its protective properties, to protect from the effects of the sun and as a sealant. 

In 2011, I moved to a house with more than average quantities of lead. Not only does it have a lead roof, but is also covered from basement to roof in lead. When I started to explore this property, located in Yorkshire, England, I soon realized that this old and quirky farmhouse had a fascinating, surprising and dark past.

Baldassare Longhena

Baldassare Longhena (1570 – 1657) was an Italian architect of the Baroque period. A pupil of the Florentine architect Francesco Borromini, he is noted for his Roman buildings, particularly the Church and Monastery of Our Lady of Loreto and the Teatro Sant'Angelo (Angel's Theatre), Bologna.
Life.
Baldassare Longhena was born and trained as an architect in Padua. As a young man, he spent some six months studying in Rome, before moving on to work with Borromini in Cremona in 1597. The collaboration between the two young architects was short, but marked Longhena's architectural approach for the rest of his life: Borromini took the more classical route, and Longhena the more decorative.
After Cremona, Longhena moved back to Padua, but his fortunes declined. In 1599, his father died, and in 1603, Longhena was thrown into prison, accused of treason. The Importance of Proper Posture

Posture is very important to our daily lives and the health of our back. Good posture means that the bones and muscles of back and neck are aligned properly when standing, sitting and lying. It means a good, normal spine position and is not simply about standing upright. In fact, there are times when the spine is relaxed and you need to bend and lean. Poor posture occurs when the spine deviates from its natural position due to weakness and imbalance in specific muscles. It also occurs when the spine is fixed in the same position for a long period of time, leading to back pain.

When you are bent forward too much, the muscles are overstretched and eventually get weakened. With this you tend to hunch over and slouch. Eventually, this will alter the normal position of the spinal nerves, causing them to pinch and interfere with their function. The nerves eventually become weakened and lose their ability to adapt with the changes in the environment that they send signals to the muscles and brain. When nerves are compromised in any way, muscle contraction and pain inevitably results. Raising awareness on disability in Africa

  • January 19, 2019

by  and 


A woman on a motorbike at sunset


It's estimated that up to 15% of people worldwide have disabilities, but the true figure is likely to be much higher because many people with impairments are not counted. The reasons for this are varied but it mainly comes down to a lack of data. In most parts of the world, a disability is not recognized as a disability unless it meets the criteria for “qualifying impairment”.


However, in these societies, there is still great need for people to understand disabilities and have conversations about ways to ensure people with impairments have access to essential public services, such as education, health-care and employment. A way to change this is to build awareness of how essential public services need to be accessible to people with impairments. If a community does not understand that it needs to take steps to make essential public services accessible to people with impairments, then the rights of people with impairments will not be fully realized and people with impairments will continue to face barriers to realizing their right to access these essential services. Once a community understands this, it can begin to take steps to achieve disability inclusion within itself.

Thirty-four subjects (17.0±1.4yrs; 12men, 22women) were recruited and had their pelvis in various positions manually during single-leg stance. Surface electromyography (EMG), was simultaneously recorded from the abductor muscles of the ipsilateral lower limb and contralateral lower limb, as they maintained the altered positions. Mean and normalized EMG of the gluteus medius, minimus, biceps femoris, vastus lateralis (VL), and the TFL was measured. The data was then compared both to baseline and between groups. The most altered position (A) when assessed in the sagittal plane consisted of a pelvic position where the pelvis was rotated back 1–4 degrees and the iliac crests moved posteriorly 1–4 degrees. What is the minimum amount of money that will end in a three digit number when written in Indian rupees?

To answer this question, you need to do a little experiment. Write down several numbers to the right of the decimal point and check which values give you three-digit numbers. You should quickly discover that all you need to do is to keep writing zeros until the number you obtain is bigger than 999.

If the first number in the number on the left is x (for instance, 999999 is 9x), then x must be greater than or equal to 1110 to make the number a three-digit number. The next number on the right, which must be a series of zeros (in the case above, xx), must then be equal to at least 111.

Therefore, 999999 is 9 x 111000, giving the maximum value for the number on the right of two zeros.
Since two zeros are not enough, the minimum value for the number on the right must be four zeros, which will give us the number on the right side. So, the minimum value is 111x, with x being greater than or equal to 1110. This gives us a thousand: the amount must be one thousand Indian rupees

Terrorism, or the threat of it, creates a sense of insecurity and a constant fear of the unknown.  It destroys our sense of freedom and undermines our trust of those around us.  Terrorism destroys our lives even when no one is injured or killed.  In some ways, this is the most frightening type.

But if we think carefully about it, it's possible for us to overcome the fear of the unknown that terrorism creates.  One way is by understanding that there are many factors that influence the likelihood of a terrorist attack.  By understanding those factors, and making sure that our actions make them less likely, we can control the terrorist's power over us.

Why Can’t I Sleep in the Daytime? The Science Behind Daytime Sleeping

A man with eyes closed on the couch while his girlfriend is sleeping behind him.

Photo by Alexandru Sava on Unsplash.

You can’t sleep in the daytime.

You try to nap, but you’re not so fortunate to get good sleep at night and you’re tired. You have the willpower to get up, get through your chores, but you’re so exhausted. When you try to have an afternoon or a midday rest, it’s too uncomfortable.

Maybe your partner can nap anywhere. They’re sleeping peacefully like a baby on the car ride to your family dinner. Or there’s a coworker who seems to do everything effortlessly. You are exhausted. If you had the right to take a nap, you would jump on that opportunity.

But you don’t. You’re not sleeping, and no one looks at you like one of the people who sleep in the daytime and can’t sleep in the night.

However, this assumption proves to be incorrect, which leads us to explore the idea of multiplication and its potential pitfalls.

Multiplication is essentially the process of repeated addition; therefore, when considering an object with multiple attributes (such as four paws), the multiplication operation allows us to account for the quantity of those respective attributes systematically. Thus, using multiplication could lead us to believe that a puppy indeed possesses eight legs since we would multiply the number of paws (four) by the number of legs each paw has (two). This mental model follows the principle of commutativity, allowing us to interchange the order of terms in the equation, ultimately yielding the same result - eight legs.

The concept of a continuous function is fundamental in calculus and analysis. To truly grasp this abstract idea, let's break it down into simpler terms, focusing on its rigorous definition while maintaining engagement through concrete examples. We will explore the necessary conditions for a function to be considered continuous at a particular point, followed by an example to illustrate these ideas concretely.

A function \(f\) defined on a domain \(\mathbb{R}\) (real numbers) is said to be continuous at a point \(x = c\) if the limit as \(x\) approaches \(c\), denoted by \(\lim_{x \to c} f(x)\), exists and is equal to \(f(c)\). Mathematically, this can be expressed as:

$$\lim_{x \to c} f(x) = f(c)$$

This implies that the values of the function \(f\) near \(c\) converge to the value of the function at \(c\). Intuitively, we can visualize this convergence by imagining a ball rolling along the graph of the function. If the ball never bounces—that is, there are no gaps or jumps in the graph—then the function is continuous at that point.

Why Is It Important to Write Term Papers?

A term paper is normally a newspaper written by senior high school or college students on a particular subject, over a period of one semester, normally accounting for roughly half of the grade. Merriam Webster defines it as a significant written assignment in an academic degree program, representing the pupil’s achievement over a year. This sort of academic writing is utilized in the end of the term, as the most important component of the student’s final assessment. It is also utilised as a tool for assessing student performance during the period of the whole semester.

The purpose of using term papers in the evaluation process is to get a holistic view of the pupil’s academic performance across a whole academic year. The evaluation of these papers is intended to measure the student’s academic skills and performance with respect to their knowledge of course material, writing skills and organization, and analytical skills and reasoning. If the student’s term papers don’t provide an acceptable degree of overall performance, then he or she will probably be considered unsuccessful at the end of the academic year.

Tribalism at Work in the United States

Most people reading this blog have a strong desire for the United States to exist as a sovereign Constitutional Republican government. As discussed in previous posts, there is much work that must be done in order to accomplish that aim, such as ending the Federal Reserve System’s monopoly on printing currency and restoring our rights under the 10th Amendment to the Constitution. However, we must also understand that there are powerful elements within this nation that seek to bring down this system of government as part of a globalist agenda. As a result, the work required to restore our Republic is all the more difficult as we have to compete against those who wish to tear down our government. If there is any silver lining to these dark times, it is that tribalism can work both ways.

One of the central themes of my last post was the issue of tribalism within the American political system. For good and for bad, people tend to support their own tribe at almost every cost. The Importance of Education on Sustainability

Education is an important key to sustainability, it is the process by which societies and individuals, especially the young are equipped to tackle today’s social problems. The most pressing issue of our time is Sustainable Development, the process by which we strive to improve our standard of living and promote environmental conservation to ensure present and future generations live up to their full potential. These efforts have been institutionalized through the United Nations with the 1987 Nairobi conference where delegates agreed to adopt measures that would foster human progress while simultaneously preserving the environment.

The 1993 UN conference held in Rio de Janeiro produced the UN Agenda 21, which is the most comprehensive set of policies that would advance sustainable development. In December 2007, countries under the United Nations agreed to a follow up programme, the Millennium Development Goals (MDG). Under these policies, countries work towards the reduction of poverty, improved healthcare for populations, promotion of education and gender inclusiveness, and better environmental management.

In Kenya for example, Sustainable Development has been the focus of recent debates given that most of the country’s developmental policies aim at improving the quality of life of Kenyans. How can I make a custom field hidden in the site collection administration page of a site?


I just wrote a custom page for site collection's Administration pages and I don't want any default field (eg. custom, document, picture , lookup etc) to appear in this page. How could I do that?

Thanks in advance.


You should remove that field's default display group using this PowerShell code or this PowerShell code:

Get-SPExceptionalField | ForEach {
    $groupId = $_.ExceptionalFields.GroupID
    $item = Get-SPFieldItem -Web $web -FieldGroup $groupId
    $item.DisplayName = ""
    Set-SPFieldGroupItem -Web $web -FieldGroup $groupId -Item $item
}

Note:

  • You can use PowerShell or PowerShell.exe if you want to execute from the SharePoint PowerShell.
  • Exceptional fields are the fields that are not configured as default fields.
  • ExceptionalFields is an SPFieldGroupItem Collection that contains all special fields.

A group of $n$ individuals meets regularly in a circle formation. Each person shakes hands with everyone else exactly once during the meeting, and no two people can shake hands with each other twice. What is the maximum number of handshakes that can occur?

As a follow-up, what is the minimum number of individuals that can participate in such an arrangement so that the maximum number of handshakes occurs?

It was already noted in a previous answer that the maximum number of handshakes is obtained when the group consists of all $n$ members of an integer ring, and that in that case the number of handshakes is precisely $n(n-1)/2$, but I would like a proper explanation for why that latter result is true.


We can solve this problem as a graph theory problem. The $V$ vertices of the graph can be the $n$ people and the edges can be the handshakes.

The maximum number of hand-shakes will be if the graph is complete, so each person has hand-shakes with everyone else, and there are no "dangling" edges.

A complete graph with $n$ vertices has $\frac{n(n-1)}{2}$ edges.

Question: What are your weaknesses?

My Answer:

Most likely every employer will ask you this question and you need to be prepared. This is the third interview question to expect, and it is not that difficult to answer as long as you are confident and prepared. As with all interview questions, this one isn't really designed to get an answer about you, however. It is designed to show a company your strengths, weaknesses, and goals, and to get you talking. This shows how well you communicate, how articulate you are, and how well you are able to think on your feet. Tropical rainforests are some of the most diverse ecosystems on the planet, and they contain an astonishing abundance of trees.

Many of these trees are unique and rare species that grow nowhere else.

But while the rainforest itself can protect these species to a degree, it is highly reliant on humans and conservation efforts to keep the rainforest secure.

Unfortunately, deforestation — the destruction of important forests due to agriculture and the sale of timber — remains a constant challenge.

The tropical rainforest biome

Tropical forests are found around the Equator.

These biomes are incredibly moist and hot, and they see more precipitation in a single year than any other ecosystem on earth.

Rainforests may vary depending on their location. For example, the rainforests in South America and Asia are generally lush and humid, often containing huge trees — such as Brazil's Brazilian Iron Wood and the Malaysian Kapok Tree — that can reach heights of 50 meters! But the rainforests in western Africa are generally less dense and more dry.

In all rainforest biomes, the trees are very dense, and many of them grow very tall to outcompete other trees and reach access to sunlight.

What if we could tell the Internet which websites are allowed to see which of our personal data, and nothing else?

That's the vision underlying a new initiative called My Data Knows Me. Started by U.S. computer scientist Paul Reiners, a professor at Washington University in St. Louis, the project is developing a new way for people to specify how their personal data can be used.

Using cryptographic tools, My Data Knows Me would enable people to set up rules about each piece of personal data they share online--for instance, rules that state "this data can be used by my bank, this data can be used by my health insurer," and so on.

Reiners hopes his tools will one day be available as an add-on feature of the next generation of Internet browsers to help people take their privacy--and their control over it--back.

CNET News spoke with Reiners about how My Data Knows Me works and how it might improve users' control over their online lives. The following is an edited transcript of the conversation.

The Importance Of A Health Insurance Plan

In recent decades, the cost of healthcare has gone up and people with medical insurance need to protect themselves, their family and their business. Health insurance, which is a type of medical insurance, is becoming an important factor in the lives of people worldwide. Insurance is a way to pay medical bills and to provide you with peace of mind. There are two types of health insurance: private and public.

Public health insurance refers to the type of health insurance that is administered by a government agency. Private health insurance can be provided by a large company or as an individual policy. There are different types of health insurance policies so you have to make a choice about which type you need.

The purpose of a health insurance plan is to help with payment of healthcare costs. Some plans will only cover certain aspects of healthcare. Some plans will pay medical costs or offer preventive care.

In a health insurance plan, the amount that is paid is based on what you pay. You can use your health insurance policy to pay for the medical care you receive. You also can use the plan to help cover the cost of medications that you can not afford.

Macedonian National Radio-Television

The Macedonian National Television and Radio (MNTR) () is the national television and radio broadcaster of North Macedonia.
History.
It was established by legislative decision on June 6, 1963 and was officially opened in September 1964. Under the name Macedonian Television the organization has been producing the television series called "Tetovo Tales" since 1967.
Before the Republic of North Macedonia gained independence, the organization was part of the Yugoslavia national television system (JRT). On 20 January 1992, after the break-up of Yugoslavia, the Macedonian parliament formed the Macedonian Television Association as a successor to JRT in Skopje. It was renamed Macedonian Radio-Television on 29 December 2001. Currently MNTR has eight stations: Skopje, Ohrid, Tetovo, Kumanovo, Gostivar, Čair, Debar, Veles and Ilinden, all of which broadcast both radio and TV programmes.

Assistant: Of course! Let's break it down step by step. We know the area of a rectangle is calculated by multiplying the length by the width. In this case, the area is 48 square units and one side (let's call it the length) is 4 units. 

So, if we set the length as 4 units, we can find the width (the other side) by dividing the area by the known length. 

Let's do the calculation: 
Area = Length * Width
48 = 4 * Width
Width = 48 / 4
Width = 12 units

So, the length of the other side is 12 units.

Riding the Waves of Change

by Heather Johnson
Lifeguard on the beach

The wave is coming.

It appears to be a harmless rolling circle out in the ocean…maybe a little larger than last time…but nothing too huge…just a normal everyday wave that people walk into and wade up to their waist. This is one of the many beautiful sights along a California beach.
Just the tiniest bit further up the waterline it gets a bit bigger and more of a worry to the person walking…how deep is it?? How fast will it come and how big will it be once it gets to him? The people running the stand down the beach are yelling…”RUN…get out of the water…another incoming wave is coming” This is a bit stressful. What does he do now?? Run and lose his towel? Walk deeper into the ocean? or will it pass on by?

Further in, the water is too deep and the wave is coming, fast. What do they do?
Ride the wave and hope that it is shallow enough to keep their head above water, or dive below and hope the wave rolls past them. The surfers are the ones who know they can beat it. The other people are scrambling out of its way

Heteropterans are predators, parasites, herbivores, and saprophytes, the order includes both beneficial and economically important pests.

Beneficial heteropterans include 18 species in 8 genera and 4 families, including 3 species of Orius, 2 species of Geocoris and 4 species of Anaphes. These species have been commercially reared and used in many parts of the world as efficient and safe biological control agents of both larvae and adults (e.g., lygus mirex, aphids).


Economic pests include 27 species in 22 genera and 10 families. This group represents a significant portion of the pestiferous Heteroptera and includes 8 species in 5 genera and 3 families in Australia, including Leptoglossus occidentalis, Dysdercus, Lygaeus, and Calycopis.

Most of these groups have an impact on Australia's agriculture, forestry and biodiversity. Leptoglossus occidentalis, a major pest of cotton, also affects tomatoes and grapes. Dysdercus, a serious pest of sugarcane in northern Australia, also causes damage to many other crops in the tropics. Lygaeus, a pest of corn

Bhavabandha

Bhavabandha () is a type of bondage in the Buddhist cosmology that is experienced by someone who has committed a bad-karma in the past. This bondage, usually is a result of bad-deeds committed in a previous life of suffering. It consists of attachment to one's past physical and mental states, which will continue to manifest themselves and further entangle the sentient being suffering from it, unless an antidote is applied.
Etymology.
"bhava" means "becoming", "to be" or "to become". "Bhavabandha" thus refers to something that binds or obstructs "bhaṇa" or "to be" from expressing itself through some kind of a phenomenon "abhava" or "not-be" that has become manifested, and hence the term.
Pratyukthabandha.
One of the twelve causes of suffering in Buddhist cosmology is "Pratyukthabandha" (), which stands for "the bondage that causes one to be unable to express one's being".
It is one of the twelve causes of suffering in the realm of the animal devas (), which encompasses both the hell-realms and the six heavenly realms of the

I've gotten to the point in my college statistics class that it's time to learn about standard deviation and the normal distribution. However, I can't seem to find a clear explanation of why standard deviation is used, why it's helpful in determining if a variable is normal, and what the relationship between standard deviation and the normal distribution curve looks like.

To add an example, I was looking at how to tell if a set of data was normally distributed (in my specific case, a set of SAT scores of ~50 students) and saw that they are normally distributed if the standard deviation is "close" to the mean score. ### Background Information

* **Identically Distributed Variables:** A sequence of random variables $\{X\_i\}\_{i=1}^n$ are said to be identically distributed if all these random variables share the same probability distribution function (pdf), denoted by $F\_{m X}}(x)$, or cumulative distribution function (cdf), denoted by $F\_{m X}}(x;{{\	ho}})$. This implies that every random variable in this sequence has the exact same set of probabilities associated with any particular value or interval. Mathematically speaking, if $X\_i {\sim} F\_{m X}}$, then $F({X\_i}\(x\)) = F\_{m X}}\(x\)$ for all $i \in {1,...,n}.$

Bulbasaur Evolution

One of the most iconic and easily recognized Pokemon, Bulbasaur would always be a fan favorite. Bulbasaur starts out with great potential, learning the Poison type attack Vine Whip and the Grass type attacks Razor Leaf and Leech Seed with high efficiency, it also learns the Normal type attack Tackle. It evolves into Ivysaur, a Grass and Poison type which learns Leech Seed earlier. Ivysaur then evolves into Venusaur, also a Grass and Poison type, and learns Solar Beam at level 20 for the Grass type. Venusaur would learn the Dragon type Dragon Claw when it reaches level 61.

Bulbasaur will always keep the move Vine Whip, as you can’t replace a learned move with Tackle. So what evolution is best for a single Tackle attack? I decided Venusaur because it learns Dragon Claw later than Ivysaur, and will always keep Leech Seed no matter what. Solar Beam also gives you a lot more options and is a powerful move. Venusaur can use Dragon Dance as well.

Linguistic anthropology

Linguistic anthropology is the study of language from a comparative cultural perspective and is concerned with how language influences social life. It is a branch of anthropology, a field in the broad study of human diversity, and linguistics, a field in the study of humanity's capacity for language. It is one of four fields of anthropology, alongside biological anthropology, cultural anthropology, and archaeology. Linguistic anthropology concerns itself primarily with the nature and organization of human linguistic systems and with the role that language plays in social life. The field often overlaps with the disciplines of sociolinguistics, pragmatics, cognitive linguistics, semiotics, anthropological linguistics, and language documentation, among others.

Linguistic anthropologists often work as descriptive linguists, attempting to describe languages that have never before been written, and analyzing how language use changes and what those changes portend for their speakers. Some also study how people use language to create group identities or solidarity (such as ethnic groups). Linguistic anthropologists often work closely with linguists in describing languages, and they are interested in how people use language in interaction with one another.

A Brief Introduction to Graph Databases

I'd like to start this off by saying that graph theory (graph databases) is a topic that I'm quite excited about, but I'm not an expert on it.

To begin, for those of you unfamiliar with graph theory, I'll give a brief introduction.

This is probably one of the best high-level explanations of graph theory that I've seen on the Internet.

A graph is defined as a set of nodes (or vertices) and a set of edges (or arcs) with an edge having an end point on each of the nodes. There are also some conventions about having directed (or undirected) edges and whether you can have multiple edges between the same nodes (multi-graphs) and whether you can have edge labels. Wikipedia does a pretty good job at laying out a list of the types of different graph definitions (the above linked article also has a good picture of what a graph looks like as well).

It makes sense that graphs are great for modelling things, and there are lots of things that graphs are useful for modelling. Some examples include cities connected by roads, friends connected by friendships, restaurants connected by their menus, and the internet!

A few weeks ago, another 40-something woman asked me, “What am I going to do with all of this fat?” She was sitting in my office surrounded by a pile of her own fat—a giant mound of liposuction leftovers. We were discussing body contouring options following massive weight loss.

This isn’t the first time that I have been asked this question, nor will it be the last.

People who lose a substantial amount of weight—60 to 100 pounds or more—often experience loose skin. For many, this loose skin can result in a loss of confidence and self-esteem. Loose, redundant skin and fat can prevent weight loss patients from fully realizing their new life goals and lead to emotional distress and mental health issues.

As a cosmetic surgeon who specializes in weight loss surgery, I work closely with dietitians and fitness experts to create a plan for my patients who are preparing to lose an extreme amount of weight.

We understand that while there is no magic cure for this excess skin, in some cases patients may want to consider surgical procedures to improve their appearance and quality of life.

All the departments belong to one of three faculties of the Free University of Berlin - the faculties of medicine, philosophy and law, and the faculty of mathematics, computer science and natural sciences.

What makes a “good” math curriculum?

Posted on December 1st, 2012 by admin


As of today, I have taught 3 different curricula: Cuisenaire Rods, Miquon Math, and Singapore Math. I know there are countless other programs out there that I have not even researched, and I imagine they all have various benefits. While I personally have not found any program to be particularly bad, I do have my own criteria (very subjective and not particularly informed, mind you) for what makes a good math curriculum. In no particular order, here are my top 3 criteria:

1) Consistency

While it is true that all students learn differently and will need different approaches to math, I also believe that consistency is important. This applies to the curriculum used and the teacher’s approach. I know, it is impossible to be totally consistent with a room full of students…but the goal should be to stay consistent as much as possible!

In my experience, a lot of the problem students have with math is a lack of consistency in instruction. Some students may not have had a good understanding of a basic concept in previous grades, and because teachers used a different approach, or did not revisit the basics often enough, those student end

This is one of our earliest and most important findings in our efforts to understand evolution.

This, my friends, is the single biggest reason I’m an atheist. There is nothing special about me that would make me so important as to be exempt from the normal laws of nature. I share common ancestry with every single life form that has ever lived and I can trace the evolutionary history of every feature on my body.

Scientists have shown recently that a 19 million year old primate fossil was found in the Dominican Republic with an amazingly preserved gut. When the contents of this fossil were studied, an intact 10 cm segment of its gut contained the feces of the common ancestor of both old and new world monkeys.[1] When they analyzed the DNA in the ancient poop, 99.9% of it belonged to the old world monkeys such as chimpanzees. So not only was this 19 million year old primate a hominid, its digestive tract belonged to the same species (99.9%!) as modern chimpanzees!

So not only have we confirmed our link to other primates, we’ve also identified one of our earliest common ancestors. We are more alike than we are different. It’s only natural that I take pride in this fact.

Pirate party of Catalonia

Pirate party of Catalonia () is a pirate party in Spain which was established in 2014 and registered on 27 July 2016. The party was founded after the electoral success of its sister party, the Pirate party of Spain, at the 2014 European Parliament election. As of 2016 the party has a membership of 1,500. It runs locally on Catalonia's party-list proportional representation system, which has historically been dominated by the socialist-led coalitions of both Catalonia and the Spanish left.
In the 2015 regional election, the party ran separately on lists in two constituencies. It polled 11,100 votes (0.1%) in the Province of Barcelona, the highest vote for a party not participating in a coalition in a constituency in the country. Its votes were below the 3% threshold for a seat, and the party was not represented at the Catalonian regional level. It was part of the Catalan party group at the Catalan parliament.
At the 2015 municipal elections the party ran candidates in nine municipalities, and got 0.3% of the vote. A 2015 law made it possible for it to get seats in the Barcelona city council.

Physicists at Purdue University working with the Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory (Fermilab, IL) in Illinois have reported that the missing solar neutrinos have been found in the lower energy "electron" form. Neutrinos are tiny, neutral, and elusive subatomic particles.

In recent years, experiments have indicated that fewer neutrinos are arriving from the sun than predicted by the standard solar model, called "Standard Solar Models."

There have been several possible explanations for the discrepancy -- that the models of the sun contain an error or that neutrinos "oscillate" from higher to lower energy forms during their 93 million-mile trip through empty space. Solving Quadratic Equations by Taking Square Root

In algebra, square root property is the property where the square root of an expression equals the product of a value and itself. In other words, square root of an expression is the value that gives a perfect square when multiplied with itself. Square root is also known as root 2 or principal root.

In solving quadratic equations by taking square root, we take advantage of the principle of equality to make one side of the equation a perfect square. It is the reverse of taking a square root. This method uses squares properties and rules to find all the roots of an equation.

Note: the radical expression is equivalent to the square root sign.

The general form of a quadratic equation is: $y=ax^{2}+bx+c$

where $a, b, c$ are constants with $a\neq 0$. The equation may be in any format: point-slope, point-form, standard form, or intercept form.

The general standard-form of a quadratic equation is: $ax^{2}+bx+c=0$

What could be worse than being forced to sit down and write an argumentative essay as a high school or college project? Well, there are more than just a few ways to make the whole exercise a complete waste of time. Not to say that these tips might have a good side somewhere, it is just that I am unable to find it. Still, they would be best left alone:

1. Never ask if “you” is acceptable for formal writing.

There is a small difference between what is good and bad for spoken English. In fact, many people have not even learned the rules of spoken English! In written English, the rules are more rigid and there are few exceptions. It is better, therefore, to ask whether it is good to use “I” in any instance, than whether it is allowed to use the informal “you.”

Never use “I” in a formal written work. It will seem very informal, almost as if you are writing to a friend or relative. Your teacher will be less than thrilled if you use “I” often in any kind of formal paper. Instead, use “one,” a third person singular noun, as the subject of your paper.

2. Remember the rules of using “you” in formal writing.

text:
I have existing web site brittlloyd.org. I want to have new domain (new domain may be like www.hello.co.uk but i want same website content. how I can redirect www.new-domain.com to www.brittlloyd.org? How can i do this?

I want same functionality I have in existing website (brittlloyd.org) in www.new-domain.com. is this possible with redirect? Please, help me.


The best option would be to use a DNS A record. For example if your old DNS A record contained

www.brittlloyd.org   192.168.1.101

you would change that to

www.new-domain.com  192.168.1.101

If you want it to appear more like a subdomain, you would need to use a CNAME record. For example

www.new-domain.com   @.

The www is optional, the @. is the same as just new-domain.com.

Redirects work too, but these are handled differently in many browsers. As such they can often look broken and they might not work properly. Using an A record or CNAME would be preferable.

text:
Are film trailers spoiling movies?
In April we talked about spoilers and whether seeing a few minutes before the opening night was beneficial to audiences.
Read that article here:
The topic of spoilers is a controversial one in the literary world.
Many people feel that spoilers do not ruin the entire experience of a book and that some are actually better if you already know what’s going to happen.
They argue that if an author’s goal was to surprise us, then they were not very good writers. A well crafted ending should always make sense, regardless of whether or not we knew what was coming. In addition to that, there are many readers who enjoy knowing the fate of a character before starting a new novel for fear they may not get to finish it once started.
However, other audiences feel knowing how a film or book ends detracts from its enjoyment by taking away surprise and suspense. They contend that anticipation is one of the most enjoyable aspects of storytelling and when you take that away for whatever reason, it reduces the overall quality of the piece.

What is it that makes a good spoiler?
The answer lies in the definition of what constitutes a spoiler. A good spoiler is one that reveals information without giving away too much; too little, and it doesn’t really serve any purpose; too much

text:
After the martyrdom of St. Boniface, the people of Frisia came under the influence of the Church and many of them became Christians. The first Bishop of Frisia was Bonifatius, and for a long time missionaries were sent to Frisia to spread the Christian faith. After the missionary work of Boniface, other missionaries in the 7th and 8th centuries took over evangelizing Frisia, including Willibrord, Lebuin, Pepin, Hilduin and Walfrid.

In the 9th and 10th centuries, a monastery was established in Eggerstedt and other religious foundations followed on the initiative of the first counts of Holland. These included a church in Terneuzen near Dordrecht. In the 12th century, the religious foundations of Terneuzen were expanded to become the first Cistercian abbey in Frisia, the first Christian monastery in the coastal area to use the Roman alphabet. The Cistercian monks of Terneuzen were known as the monks of the white clothes (due to their white dress uniform). It is possible to erase up to 4 HDDs at a time. This makes it much easier to erase files that your hard drive may hold that you’d like to dispose of securely.

Once you erase a drive properly, all data is erased from it with no way to undo it. This means you won’t accidentally open up the files that were there before.

The only way to get back data after it is erased is to recover it after erasing the drive. And it takes advanced expertise and tools to do this.

If you’re selling a device, such as a laptop or mobile phone, you can erase the data and then wipe down the hard drive with something like Windex or alcohol to remove any fingerprints and signs that it used to belong to you.

You don’t usually need to take steps to erase your phone after selling it. The next owner will probably do that on their own, but it doesn’t hurt to help them out.

There are some other tools you can use for data erasure. For instance, you can use something like CCleaner for your computer’s files. It’s important to note that this method of erasing data is only meant for files that are still on your hard drive. It doesn’t have a way to erase an entire drive

What can UAV do?

To a great extent, the main task of each Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) is mapping. With its help, you can create a detailed 3D map of any area, taking into account the terrain, topographic features and architectural. It can be a topographic map, a detailed digital map or the final project for architectural designing.

How does the map differ from those made with a satellite and a helicopter?

The difference is in the resolution. The UAS system helps to create a map with a clear resolution in several meters, which is impossible with satellites and helicopters. It is also capable of creating detailed map sections (for example, the territory of a stadium), which cannot be done with satellites and helicopters. UAS allows you to record not only terrain elevation, but also any elements: buildings, objects, etc.

A number of similar laws have already passed around the country, and the District's Board of Education passed an emergency rule Friday night making it illegal for students to use cellphones while driving school buses. (Ald. Mary Cheh is pushing for a similar move in the District.)

Distracted driving has been a major campaign issue for County Executive Steve Vaughan, who was nearly hit by a car while driving across the Fourteenth Street Bridge in 2011. More than 10 percent of the 60 fatal accidents on the Capital Beltway in 2011 involved cellphone use, according to the Arlington County Department of Transportation.

text:
So you have your children writing every day. Great. You have them write about the things they did, and in the case of a homeschool family, you can have them write about what they accomplished during the school day and how it went. I do suggest writing every school day. There just isn’t enough time in the week to accomplish everything, but I will talk about that more in a minute. The next step is to incorporate writing into every day life. Instead of just having your children write about what they did, have them write about it while it is happening. Have them write about what it is like going on a field trip. Have them write about meeting someone famous, or even a not so famous person. Write about a conversation you overheard. Describe a smell without saying what the smell is, then let the reader guess. Describe a feeling, but don’t say what the feeling is, let them guess. You can also find pictures in old magazines, cut them out and stick them on the wall or refrigerator and let your children write a story about the picture. Or get them to describe the picture.

Another great idea for writers, especially younger writers, is to have them go on a scavenger hunt. Tell them to go and write ten things they find interesting

text: Both the federation of states and the federal super-state of the Soviet Union were formed with the express intention to ensure mutual cooperation and to secure the rights of the republics and autonomous areas within the union. The Soviet Union consisted of 15 Soviet Socialist Republics. For three of these—the Estonian, Latvian and Lithuanian Republics—national sovereignty was established soon after the fall of the Soviet Union. The other republics have been given varying degrees of autonomy. The Russian Federation, which was the largest constituent republic of the USSR, is one such member. Other republics such as Ukraine and Belarus have become fully independent, while others such as Uzbekistan have declared independence but remain under strong Russian influence, and others such as Tajikistan and Armenia are not recognized by the whole international community. The Soviet Union had a great effect on world culture, for instance by creating the world's second most used alphabet which was used as the alphabet for many languages across the world, and by inventing certain art forms such as Socialist Realism, the music of Shostakovich, and films like the sci-fi movie Metropolia. The topological phase of matter, in which the low energy excitations of the system are described entirely by the geometry of the system rather than its detailed underlying potential, is one of the most exciting fields in theoretical physics today. The first topological phases of matter, known as quantum Hall and quantum topological phases with particles described by nonrelativistic Hamiltonians, were well understood but only studied in two spacetime dimensions. At the same time, it was also believed that, due to a certain mathematical obstruction, there was no way to define fermions on a lattice in 2+1 dimensions and have topologically protected, robust edge excitations. Thus, no 2+1D topological theory with local, nondegenerate fermionic ground states was known. The results were a large study that followed around 1100 participants, aged around 60 years old for two years. Both the men and women completed sleep tests as well as providing blood or breath samples so the researchers could check their blood sugar and insulin levels, and hormone levels.

The study found no association at all between sleep and eating, other than late-night or night-time snacking. The reason for this is thought to be because people who are snacking at night time don’t associate this with meals, and generally snack more because foods they eat are more calorie and fat heavy due to convenience. This means that because the research was investigating meal times, instead of snacking, it did not uncover how bad bedtime snacking can be for your sleep quality.

What is the Difference Between Physical Affairs and Emotional Affairs?

Physical affairs are affairs that involve two people having sex. On the contrary, emotional affairs do not involve sex at all. They are situations where one person forms an emotional attachment with another person outside of marriage. In the United States, in the state of Utah, both physical affairs and emotional affairs can lead to the annulment of a marriage.

In certain situations, the law allows the parties to seek a divorce through fault grounds. Fault grounds are used instead of no-fault grounds. In Utah, the grounds for divorce in most cases are no-fault, but in some cases, fault grounds can be used and adultery or desertion can be cited. In addition, in Utah, there is the legal separation option as an alternative to divorce. text:
A special indicator instead of aquarium water permits direct measurement of nitrogenous substances. To carry out the measurement, water is mixed with an indicator, a liquid containing reagents that change their color in response to the presence of ammonia, nitrite and nitrate. To detect the indicator, there is a set of test tubes — test tube, sample, reagent indicator tube and a reagent. The sample of aquarium water should be taken directly from the aquarium, the volume of which should not be more than 10 ml, and it is better if it is less - not more than 2-3 ml.

An aquarium with fish
An aquarium with fish

To detect ammonia, nitrite and nitrate, you need to add, in sequence, one reagent to the sample: the first is the reagent ammonia, second nitrite, third nitrate. For each sample, there is a corresponding tube with the color of the indicator at the end of the measurement. With the addition of the reagent to the indicator tube, a change in the color will occur and the degree of color intensity indicates the level of the compound in the aquarium water. In the case of ammonia, the water color will change from a light to a darker pink. With higher concentrations of nitrites
prompt_prefix: A special indicator instead of aquarium water permits direct measurement

Question 1. Is there a concern for a spike in the disease rate this spring? Answer: In some regions, the disease rate will undoubtedly be higher compared to last fall, while in other areas the disease rate will be low based on the specific disease concerns and how dry the ground was for a number of days following the rains. As stated previously, we had a late fall and thus a shorter time period for grass to dry out, but that also means there tends to be a less likelihood of high level of disease.

Question 2. Are diseases worse on fields that were wetter for a longer period? Answer: When a disease is worse on any given athletic field this year, it could be due to one or more of the following factors – 1) location and proximity to the field to water; 2) how much and how long the stand was under water; 3) type of ground cover that the turfgrass was covered by prior to the start of the season; 4) presence or absence of thatch; 5) quality of the grass

Is downloading from torrent sites illegal in the USA? Why?

A lot of people download stuff from torrent sites every day, it’s no big deal anymore, right?

Well, it’s still technically illegal in the USA, even though people do it anyway with impunity. Why?

The legal situation is actually not so clear cut that you can be prosecuted. It’s more like a grey area which might or might not hold up in court.

Is downloading from torrent sites illegal in the USA? Why? (1)

You are unlikely to be prosecuted if you only download for your own use and you don’t do heavy volume of illegal downloading. However, you shouldn’t just assume that it’s no big deal. There are a bunch of reasons why it is still illegal.

The Legal Situation Regarding Torrent Sites

Torrents are a way of distributing data files. Torrenting is a method of uploading and downloading digital files using a peer-to-peer (P2P) file-sharing system. In other words, it is a method of sharing files among multiple users over the internet by downloading and uploading parts of each file simultaneously. The user then puts the pieces together like a jigsaw puzzle.

Legal downloads for free

There are legal torrent websites which help with legal downloads for free.

Why I don't eat meat

"I didn't really give up meat, it gave up me. I started to suffer from migraines and indigestion, and when, after six months of experimentation, my GP confirmed the link, I stopped eating meat. It was a bit of an anti-climax as I'd already decided not to, really, but I'm relieved not to have all those worries and regrets about buying meat or fish. We need to think about meat more, why and where it comes from. Meat is also bad for the environment. In Britain, 70% of fresh water goes into agriculture, and much of that goes into rearing livestock. Meat production also creates global warming gases and contributes to eutrophication — the process whereby too much nitrogen in waterways leads to massive plant growth and lack of oxygen. Sperm Whales are dark brown in colour and can dive to depths of more than a mile in less than half an hour.

They feed on cephalopods, which include octopuses, squid, and the giant squid! Though this creature is enormous (one giant squid could weight 450 kg), it’s small compared to the sperm whales favorite food, the colossal squid, which can be up to 15 metres in length! text:
Chronic Kidney Illness (CKD) is an umbrella term used to diagnose when chronic kidney disease (CKD) has a lower but stable eGFR (estimated glomerular filtration rate) and is also referred to as stage 4 or 5 CKD. An estimated 1.4 million people may be living with early chronic kidney disease worldwide. Chronic kidney disease is the ninth leading cause of death globally. 1

People in low and middle-income countries, including Bangladesh, may be at risk as they may develop chronic kidney disease due to a combination of factors, such as high blood pressure, diabetes and infectious diseases. The symptoms of chronic kidney disease usually do not appear until it has reached an advanced stage, making it difficult for diagnosis. 2

Symptoms of chronic kidney disease are usually caused by a build-up of waste products in the blood and an imbalance of minerals in the body, leading to a loss of energy, lack of appetite, nausea, dry and itchy skin and an increased risk of infections. People with chronic kidney disease may be at higher risk of having a stroke or a heart attack. 2

Diabetics have high blood sugar levels. High blood sugar, along with high blood pressure, can lead to diabetic kidney disease
prompt_prefix: Chronic Kidney Illness (CKD)

These include improvements to the A8 dual carriageway, an extension to the town's bypass road (now called the A8 dual carriageway), a new access overbridge on the B764 by-pass road and a new link road from the A1 road to the A8 by-pass road to allow traffic to bypass the town.
In August 2006 a new by-pass road, the A8 bypass, was opened by the Queen as part of the Queen's Golden Jubilee celebrations.   • Failing to report to the probation officer

What a Probation Officer Wants to See
A probation officer’s goal is to ensure that you abide by the terms of your probation when you are released from prison. This can mean doing things such as going to all community service appointments, paying restitution, completing drug or alcohol treatment, and seeking mental health services. While meeting all of those requirements will ensure you are not breaking the rules of your probation, you still want to make sure you show your probation officer all of the positive changes you are making to become a law-abiding citizen again.

What a probation officer really wants to see from their client is that the client takes responsibility for what they did wrong and does whatever it takes to stay out of trouble in the future. If you show your good side to the probation officer and take your probation seriously, it will go a long way toward ensuring that the probation officer will vouch for your efforts.

Instead of going back to work, Kevin will likely spend the rest of his life using a wheelchair. He’ll spend his days in the hospital or a rehab facility, unable to move on his own. He won’t be able to have the family and social relationships he once enjoyed since many of his friends and family don’t know how to relate to and support a quadriplegic. Most importantly, he won’t be financially independent and will face an incredibly high medical care costs for the rest of his life.

Want to enjoy your drink at any time? Apparently it’s possible to create a smartphone app that can read your emotions.

Researchers at the University of Rochester have come up with the technology that will recognize your level of excitement from the rhythm of your heart beats. Using Bluetooth headphones that transmit heartbeat data to an app, the software will analyze your pulse rate while you consume your drink.

While there are several existing techniques for analyzing human emotional states from electrocardiograms (ECG), this is the first research effort to show that changes in the autonomic nervous system are sufficient to measure a person’s emotions accurately.

With the app, your emotions can be classified into one of five different states – pleasure, arousal, fear, disappointment, relaxation – and you can see how they change while you consume your drink. The research was published in the IEEE Signal Processing Letters journal.

“Now our system is more sensitive to detect a person’s pulse with smartphone devices,” said Yongguang Huang, a research associate of the University of Rochester’s Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering.

“We believe the system can offer an interesting experience, which can improve the user experience of a mobile device.”

Folding dining chairs usually have metal frames with webbing and upholstered seats, or wooden frames with upholstered seats. Wood chairs are the simplest in design and construction. Dayton WordCamp | The Jamestown Comet.
This is one of the rare cases where I'm writing about my own writing. But it's not to toot my horn ... I promise.

The James River Writers is a literary organization in Richmond, VA that I've been a member of the past two years. As a member, I've gotten the chance to hang out with authors I've admired as well as connect with other writers on a personal level. It's been a blast.

A few weeks ago, one of the monthly meetings for WRW was devoted to a topic I knew nothing about: web design and social media. This was a challenge since I'm an English major and I barely know more about technology than the average adult.

So I put on my big girl panties and signed up to speak at a Writer's Workshop. I talked about Facebook, WordPress and Twitter and learned so much that has helped me promote my writing and build my platform (oh, how the lingo has grown!).

The feedback has been overwhelmingly positive, and the WRW president has been encouraging me to give a talk at the upcoming Dayton WordCamp in November. I'll be talking about blogging as a writer. I can't think of a better way to start networking than to attend

  • They can automatically download, install, and maintain software packages, their dependencies and updates. This makes it very easy for even a complete Linux newby to install and use a Linux distribution and all of its software packages.

  • The package dependencies mechanism ensures that the packages are consistent enough to reduce the number of software conflicts that could lead to unexpected problems.

Many Linux distributions and applications use the same package management systems. text:
WBS: why is it important & why it works

A work breakdown structure is one of the most important tools, developed many years ago, and still in use today, for managing large projects. The purpose of the work breakdown structure (WBS) is to decompose the project work into manageable parts. The objective is for these parts to be clear, specific, and manageable for the team.

The WBS should take into consideration the organization’s culture and how the team works.


WBS is a way of expressing information about the work that needs to be done. It is a communication tool. It allows everyone on the team to stay on the same page with regard to the work. Without the work breakdown structure, teams can waste time and energy by not knowing exactly what they need to get done.

Benefits of the WBS

The work breakdown structure (WBS) provides the following benefits for the team member.

  1. It’s a visual aid that allows the team to clearly see the entire project.
  2. It gives the team a reference as to when an aspect of the project has been completed.
  3. It helps the team to understand clearly the deliverable they are working toward.
  4. It prevents the team from being overwhelmed

Emergency situations at petrol filling stations are not so uncommon, as there may be a shortage of fuel or even a fire.

Emergency situations at petrol filling stations are not so uncommon, as there may be a shortage of fuel or even a fire. In such situations, you should not hesitate to inform the service station manager as soon as possible about the dangerous situation.

The first step is to notify the manager at the gas station about the situation. If the petrol truck has not arrived at the gas station yet, you should move the vehicle some distance away from the pumping station where it will not interfere with the fuel delivery team. 

The service station team members may need access to the vehicle to carry out the necessary repairs and prepare the fuel tank for the delivery crew to refill. If the fuel tanker has already arrived and was filling the tanks at the gas station when the accident occurred, all cars should be moved away from the station immediately. In this case, the pumping team can continue with the discharge of the fuel without interference.

Once the situation has been contained, the next step will be to report the crash to the police or firefighters who will arrive at the scene within a short time. In urgent situations, it’s better to use a cellphone, as it’s convenient and fast communication tool

 In the 1960″s a group of enthusiasts known as the ‘Museum Society’ formed in Wetherby and set about restoring and preserving a number of important artifacts and buildings, some of which are still housed in the museum at Wetherby library. They also began to create a list of the known buildings in Wetherby, which would later become known as the 'Museum Society List', and the earliest known of these dates from 1968. The society, originally comprising of 20 members who formed into 7 committees, continues to run today, but not in the capacity which it was created, now more commonly known as the Wetherby Heritage Group.


The society was made up of a mixture of individuals from the professional sector, tradespersons, housebuilders, the clergy, business people, artists and craftsmen, and the aim of the society was to create a society to ensure the history, buildings and characters of old Wetherby were not forgotten.

Older people still think of the park as a nice place to walk through. They like the big trees, the open spaces and the green grass. So do many young people. But nowadays, most children do not play there.
Parents are worried about things like traffic danger and stranger danger. So they often don't let their children play outside until they are old enough to understand about danger, or are old enough to stay at home with a babysitter. Sometimes the only people in the park are grandparents with their grandchildren.
But some parents think it is important for their children to play outdoors. They take them to play areas in the park when they are very young. If parents want to, they can still find places in the park where it is safe for little children to play.

text:
Whether it's because I've experienced a natural disaster on a large scale, or because I grew up as a kid during severe drought years, I've always felt a special connection to water.

But you do not need to experience catastrophic weather events to realize the importance of this resource in our lives. Water covers three-fourths of the surface of the Earth. We need water for drinking, to grow crops, and to maintain wildlife. But fresh water is a finite resource. At any given time, 97,200 liters of water is in the atmosphere, 8,700 liters sits above the Earth's surface (on land and in rivers and oceans) and 3,500 liters remains underground. Yet more than one million people die every year because they do not have access to clean water for drinking. As I write this essay in December 2015, it is hard to believe we are still dealing with this crisis.

In addition to the lack of clean water for drinking, we humans need much more water for agriculture. Approximately 70% of our use of clean water goes to growing crops. It takes about 1,000 liters of water to produce one kilogram of wheat.

It has been estimated by scientists that if we want to feed some 9 billion people in

While Venus has long puzzled scientists, there remains a great deal to discover about its landscape and its atmosphere. The mission would investigate the planet’s strange weather and the way that it interacts with Venus’ surface.

“The scientific rationale for choosing Venus is that it is, in our solar system, the most similar planet to Earth in terms of mass, size and composition,” David Rothery, an expert in planetary geosciences at the Open University in the United Kingdom, said in an ESA video announcing the mission. “So it allows us to get our foot into the door of exploring the processes [on] planets that have active geology in their past.”

manzanita plant

What are the characteristics of Manzanita?

Manzanita is a bush found in the Northern Hemisphere. It's a very adaptable plant, capable of surviving many different types of soil and weather conditions. Its leaves are shiny and green year-round, making it an attractive evergreen. It can grow up to ten feet tall but is usually much smaller than that.

Where is the best place to locate Manzanitas?

This plant is found in the mountains of North America, including all of the western United States. It thrives in a dry, rocky environment and requires very little water. For best results, you will need to plant your manzanitats in sandy soil that has some rocks mixed in.

What kinds of Manzanitas are grown?

There are several types of manzanitas available. You can buy a manzanita bush at any local garden center in the United States. However, you can get higher-quality manzanitas in a greenhouse that grows and cares for them specifically for their beauty.

text:
The Western Shoshone call themselves Newe, a name which means “human being” in their own language. Although these native people lived throughout the Nevada territory for generations, their history was interrupted in 1863 when gold was discovered. The United States declared the area open for mining, without consulting the Shoshone about their territory. The Shoshone had no right to vote, no right to represent themselves, and no legal protection of their rights to the land they had lived in for so long.

In 1868, the United States government forced the Shoshone to sign the Treaty of Ruby Valley, which transferred all title to the Shoshone people to the United States. In exchange, the United States gave the Shoshone $5,000 annually and assigned them a small area of reservation land in southeastern Nevada. This was a fraction of their total territory and the $5,000 was a pittance compared to their resources. This treaty was also signed without the Western Shoshone’s consent.

In 1969, the United States terminated the $5,000 payments to the Shoshone. The Shoshone had no alternative but to turn to mining the land which the United States had stolen from them, though they did not have adequate resources
prompt_prefix: The Western Shoshone call themselves Newe,

That food pyramid is based on the Institute of Medicine’s 2006 dietary reference intake guidelines (DRI). They recommend that the general population eat 6-11 servings of grains every day. The NFCA and IOM do not differentiate between gluten-free grains and all grains. This dietary recommendation is not evidence-based for those with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity.

The main problem for people with celiac disease is that many gluten-free grains are still considered whole grains. If you have celiac disease, please note that the following grains (whole or otherwise) should be avoided:

  1. Cereals: cereal flakes/oats, bran, rice bran
  2. Flours: All-purpose gluten-free flour, tapioca/tapioca starch, amaranth flour, millet meal, oats, oat bran, oatmeal, rice flour, wheat gluten, wheat bran

text:
It was time to pack up camp and head north for a few nights rest and to get away from the constant noise. So, before dark, we made a small base camp at the mouth of one of the canyons and went to bed early.

We were woken on the first of June by a loud boom far to the northwest. It rolled through the forest for a long time. It sounded like a cannon had been fired. We were looking straight at the direction of the sound and nothing could be seen. The noise echoed back and forth through the mountains, and finally faded away far to the northwest.

It was mid afternoon when we saw the first of several large flocks of ducks flying overhead. They were coming in from the northwest, probably from the Alaska Peninsula, and there were hundreds of them. They were flying quite high, much higher than most of the birds we had seen, and they were coming from straight overhead. Every once in a while, we could see some of the birds far in the distance with binoculars.

A few days later, we were sitting in camp on a sunny afternoon and we saw some more ducks coming towards us from the northwest. They were several thousand feet up and headed right for the valley we were in. We were watching
prompt_prefix: It was time to pack up camp and head north

text: SRID is a tedious and time consuming assay to perform. We therefore investigated the possibility of using PCR methods to rapidly detect a range of viruses which cause haemorrhagic disease in sheep (HDV, BDV, CSFV and PPRV) and compared the PCR methods to the SRID in order to establish their usefulness as a diagnostic tool. Results demonstrated that PCR could discriminate between viruses using the virus-specific 5' primer with varying amplification efficiencies ranging from 83.3%-104%. The primers and amplification times were established to allow discrimination between viruses in the most commonly infected sheep breeds while using a thermocycler with a default 5 minute hold at 95oC. However, while the primers are highly specific for each virus and have no cross reactions with each other, they do give spurious amplification when using non-specific DNA as a template. However, despite this problem, the assay could be used to determine if a sheep was virus negative for all four viruses. The rapidity, sensitivity and specificity of PCR offers the prospect of a cheap, simple assay that would replace the expensive and more tedious SRID to enable the rapid diagnosis, and thus treatment of virus-infected sheep.

We are working in $\Bbb Z/2\Bbb Z$ here. So, for a number $n$ we need an equivalence class of elements to cycle through with only one element left uncovered as the base case.

So, we can let $n=2^k$ where $k\in\Bbb Z$, and take any representative of each class modulo $2^k$. We then take only the representatives that aren't already covered in their entirety.

For $k=1$ we have representatives $1$ and $2$. And we have to exclude $2$, so $1,3,5,\dotsc$ is the result.

Our checking is different than bank checking. It’s called “checking” because it’s like a check. After you have deposited a check, it can take a few days to clear and be available. Some people think they have that money to spend right away, and they write a check on it. That “floating” check can cause serious trouble.

That floating check can be written to another merchant, or even to your bank, by mistake. Then that merchant might not get paid.

What’s worse is that if the merchant doesn’t get paid, then that could bounce back and affect your local merchants — especially your gas station! For example, you may think there’s money in your account to pay the Chevron gas station, but you wrote a check to the local florist, thinking it was the credit union, and that’s why your Chevron was charged back.

That happens frequently enough to put your local merchants at risk of losing money. If it happens too often, it could cause a merchant to stop doing business with the credit union.

Your credit union is very proud of the work we do in our community. Please take extra care to review your checking account balances, and don’t write any checks, including to the credit union, if you aren’t sure the check will clear

The NFPA 1404 Standard on Firefighter Training and Evaluation published in 1988, identified three methods of fire training: real-time, live-fire and simulation. While simulated burn training was the preferred method, live fires presented the danger of injury and fire spread, not a viable option for most training agencies. text: A phase II pilot study to evaluate use of intravitreal injection of pegylated recombinant human vascular endothelial growth factor (rh-VEGF) into the subretinal space of eyes with non-exudative wet age-related macular degeneration has been completed at the University of Texas in San Antonio (UTSA). In addition to pharmacodynamic parameters of rh-VEGF and biomarkers of retinal disease, we also evaluated the intra-subretinal distribution of rh-VEGF over 21 to 30 days in five treated subjects. The authors found that rh-VEGF penetrated the retina within 30 minutes; however, it was not detectable after 24 hours. Subsequent dosing with rh-VEGF significantly increased rh-VEGF at the RPE and overlying neural retina to levels detectable at the interface with the vitreous cavity by up to 14 days and at the subretinal space by up to 21 days, without a dose-dependent increase in systemic bioavailability. What is an atom, exactly?

The most fundamental part of an atom is the nucleus. This contains the subatomic particles called protons and neutron. A proton has a positive electric charge and a neutron has no electric charge. The protons and neutrons stick together to form a massive central core in the atom called a nucleus. The nucleus is surrounded by electrons, which have a negative electric charge. It is the electric charge of the protons that is responsible for the nucleus being positively charged, and the electrons that are negatively charged.

The electric force acts on the positive charges, causing them to "repel" each other and to seek the nearest negatively charged particle, the electrons. So the electrons are attracted to the nucleus. In most atoms, the electrons orbit the nucleus in shells. The electrons in each shell of an atom have specific "shells". We’ll look at the energy of these shells in our next video.

text: system requires only 12 seconds to open or close. When the shutter is open, the two sides of each panel move laterally in opposition to expose about 60 percent of the panel. These two panels are separated by a narrow slit or a slit that widens to allow for continuous scanning, depending on the mode of operation. The two panels are kept in opposition by an electric motor, driven by a synchronous motor, one being fed out of phase by a phase-shifting gearbox. The slit can be in any position along a horizontal line between the two opposing panels. A continuous vertical scan is accomplished by a rotating mirror, placed behind the slit, whose axis is at right angles to the slit, and which is moved from side to side by a stepper motor. The slit and the mirror are synchronized at a speed of 300 lines per second. The camera lens is mounted at the end of the slit. A camera shutter is therefore not required if the mode of operation is for long-term observation. The aperture can be restricted by a narrow slit between 2 and 3 mm wide.
