An inventory advertisement allows a continuous intake of applications, even before a suitable candidate has been identified. It is a very appropriate way of ensuring that candidates who are not aware of the opening and do not have access to the information that leads them to respond immediately to an advertisement, will have an opportunity to apply when and if the opening becomes available. An open advertisement allows more persons to become aware of opportunities and this may lead to the recruiting of a better qualified candidate.

The advantages of this method of advertising are that the vacancy is not restricted to one group or one locality. The possibility of discrimination in the employment process is reduced. The job announcement is accessible to those who read newspapers in the locality where the vacancy is available or who may be referred by third parties.

It is however sometimes argued that the recruitment of a person who is not best suited for a position will be counter-productive to the organization. In such a case an advertisement for a person who will be most effective in that position has to be seen in the long-term as the optimum option.

In 2005 HBOS established a savings platform under the Halifax name. In 2019, under the newly merged bank name Nationwide, it was rebranded for a second time as NatWest Savings.

The modules for each of these disciplines are taught over two separate semesters, allowing for a deeper understanding of the subject as a whole.
It is intended to be more of a research degree rather than a vocational one. The majority of the course modules involve students conducting in-depth independent legal research with supervisors. A typical module requires the students to write their own report and to present the findings in a seminar. An example of such a seminar is the International Justice seminar.
Students must conduct a dissertation, with a minimum of 12,000 words, in their third and final year. Students must also study and complete a professional skills module, which includes: oral presentation & communication, legal research, commercial awareness, legal terminology, and critical thinking.
The undergraduate course is considered less research based. Students undertake a full-time course, with around 80% law and 20% non-law.

text:
I met with Secretary of the Air Force Heather Wilson and she took this idea very seriously,” Nellis said. “They had an air-to-air test last year and we’re hoping for an inoperational test soon of the technology.”

Nellis has been working with NASA on hypersonic aircraft technology since 2003, when the agency hired the engineer away from Boeing, where he was helping develop hypersonic capabilities for military bombers and reconnaissance planes. He was also with NASA’s predecessor, Langley Research Center when he designed a 5-inch long device in 1997 for the agency’s X-43 hypersonic aircraft.

The device, called the “coanda nozzle,” has a cone attached to a thin film that separates air moving at supersonic speed from air moving at subsonic speed. The concept was first demonstrated in the past century by Romanian aerodynamics pioneer Henri Coanda.

The device, now being researched at Nellis for incorporation into various hypersonic engines, increases lift across a wide range of speeds and allows the aircraft’s engines to work efficiently at high speeds.

While hypersonic technology on its own is not new, what makes the technology being developed at Nellis unique is the range of technologies currently being tested, Nellis said.

An orca (Orcinus orca), also known as a killer whale, is a species of toothed whale. It is the largest member of the oceanic dolphin family (Delphinidae).  

The orca's anatomy is similar to that of other dolphins but is more robust, has a larger head and bigger teeth.
How Do Orcas Get Their Names? 

Orcinus is a taxonomic genus name, derived from the Latin word orca: whale of a frightful form, perhaps derived from the Greek word, orkos (ὄρκος), 'wolf'. Residential buildings account for a significant amount of the world’s energy consumption. Most of this energy can be traced to fossil energy sources which when burned, release carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. These greenhouse gases contribute to global warming.

A major way to reduce these carbon emissions is through the use of alternative or renewable energy sources, a strategy referred to as decarbonization or decarbonisation. Many European countries are making an aggressive effort to achieve net zero emission energy by 2050. A major way to reach this goal is to increase the use of renewables within the energy grid. One of the main challenges to widespread renewable energy adoption is the variability of renewable energy generation depending upon weather conditions and time of the day.

An increasing portion of the energy supply grid will therefore need to be either energy storage as well as power balancing capacity to manage the intermittency of renewables. One method to achieve this that is also financially attractive for building owners is through energy storage for onsite renewable production along with power balancing capacity at a utility scale level.

text:
On of the key elements for most cloud solutions and containers to scale is the ability to distribute your code or application across multiple nodes. This is how you get horizontal scalability for your applications. However, it’s crucial that any distributed system has a mechanism to keep data in sync between all these nodes in your cluster as you add more nodes. Without syncing your data across the network of nodes that make up your system, all you would have is distributed processing, not distributed system. This is the same thing as distributing data between multiple microservices, instead of one monolithic database that your app relied on before.

What Is a Distributed Database Layer?

You need a database layer that is specifically designed to store data across multiple nodes in a data center in real time. It works the same as any other relational database but has been tuned specifically for a distributed environment. A distributed database layer ensures that every query against the database always returns the same result regardless of where it’s running. No matter how many nodes you add, the database layer remains distributed and works the same as the day you started.

What is a database sharding?

Last summer I took a cross-country road trip with my husband. It was hot, but we had the windows down and the radio playing so we could escape the heat and stress of the city. Our last stop before our destination was in the small town where one of us grew up. When we arrived, we were both shocked to find the gas station closed and nearly every store boarded up. As we drove through this town, I thought about what it must be like to call a place you once lived home but find that there’s nothing left. How does one live when there is no home, no job, no community? How do you find peace with yourself and your purpose when you’re the only one remaining who cares in a place that once was? How do you grieve for the people and places you love when you don’t know how to leave the burden behind?

This is an important question that haunts me, especially in my professional life as a social worker. While the term “dying of grief” may seem extreme, many of my clients are living through a kind of chronic grief that is affecting their relationships, work, and general well-being. They grieve in their hearts, knowing what it used to be and what it could have been, never fully reconc

Q: What is a cookie?

A: Cookies are small text files or identifiers that are sent to your computer by a Web server. Cookie information is stored directly on your computer’s hard drive and not on the Web servers. Cookies allow Web sites to collect information about how often you visit, what pages you look at, how long you stay on a page, what you look at, where you come from, and where you go next. The cookie can include a unique identifier, allowing companies to track your movement within their site, and over multiple sites. It is this capability that the FTC is considering regulating.


Q: Do cookies pose a privacy threat?

A: That depends on the cookie, and where and by whom it is used. A cookie can contain anything from a session token to your user preferences. Police Brutality - ACTS Law Firm in California - Civil Rights Wrongdoing - Police Misconduct - Racial Bias

Police Brutality

California Civil Rights lawyers representing victims of police misconduct and brutality from police officers of all state and local law enforcement agencies throughout California, including the Los Angeles and San Francisco police departments as well as the local police in San Diego, Sacramento, Irvine, Anaheim, San Jose, Santa Cruz, Hollywood, Bakersfield, Long Beach, Compton, Fresno, and Riverside.

Police BrutalityWhat Is Police Misconduct?

Police misconduct occurs when a police officer does something against a citizen that violates the citizen’s constitutional rights. When officers violate another person’s rights by using excessive force, it is known as “police brutality.”

The types of police misconduct that you may observe as police brutality are:

Excessive force: Police officers may use force in any situation, but in doing so, should use only a level of force required to make an arrest.

For example, if a police officer pulls over a driver for speeding and the driver acts nervously, and resists the officer by putting up her hands, the officer cannot shoot the driver. Officers should also not use excessive force by beating up a suspect unnecessarily after he has already been arrested or subdued.

Sugar And Our Kids: Are We Doing Enough?
Do you know what the maximum amount of added sugar that FDA recommends for a child over the age of two to consume per day? It’s 6 teaspoons. 

While the total calories allowed for kids ages 2-18 range from 1400 for a 3 year old to 2500 calories for an 11 or 12 year old, the FDA does recommend that less than 5% of their total calories come from added sugar. While they don’t recommend the sugar content be limited, they do mention that the less added sugar a child eats, the less likely he will be to develop weight and tooth issues.

So what is added sugar? It is the sugar added to foods during processing (like cereal, ketchup, etc.), but does not include the naturally occurring sugar that is contained in certain fruits, vegetables, dairy products, etc. and is needed as a part of a healthy, balanced diet. 

So is 6 teaspoons a day too many? Probably.
When foods with added sugar are allowed too often and in large amounts, added sugar is responsible for many health problems. Obesity is first and foremost on this list, and obesity in children is rising quickly, but is not the only reason

National parks are typically established to conserve the most spectacular and scenic areas of a country. National parks have a broad social mission to conserve, protect, and maintain natural resources and wildlife in a particular ecosystem.

2.2.4 Selection of flow path

The flow path selection is a complex task, because many different process options can be used, but only one combination is best, considering process economics and quality requirements.  In practice, it is virtually impossible to model all the possible combinations of unit processes and equipment configurations, particularly taking all processing options into consideration.  It is often recommended that, once an early, preliminary design is complete, it is best simply to choose a “straight-forward” processing flow sheet and proceed with further design based on it.

Each year, there are more than 100,000 earthquakes around the globe. About 20 of these are felt by a few people around their source; the other 80,000+ are so tiny that only sensitive seismographs can detect them. In fact, earthquakes are happening every minute of every day with the majority of them undetectable, undamaging and even unknown. A significant number of earthquakes are felt at their epicenter (the source of the earthquake and directly above their hypocenter) - and many are felt in many nearby locations.

Most earthquakes are located along fault zones, which are cracks in Earth's crust along which rocks move (in the case of Earth, these are always in the lithosphere or crust - and not the mantle!). A fault zone can be as narrow as a few centimeters to hundreds of kilometers. In addition to the San Andreas Fault, other well-known fault lines around the world include the San Andreas fault in California; the Pacific plate in Asia; the Dead Sea, which separates Africa and Asia; the New Madrid Fault in the United States; and volcanoes in Iceland, Japan and the Philippines.

The aim of the rice field project is to raise the social capital in rural areas. Social capital is the way people build relations with each other within their communities and their networks and use them at the right time. It encompasses the social networks, norms, and collective and personal actions to increase value for and within the community in order to enhance the individual and community members’ ability to meet personal requirements and their ability to participate in the wider social and community life. Caused by pituitary adenomas, ischemic damage, other tumors, or metastases, secondary hypopituitarism may result from surgical removal, radiation treatment, or damage to the pituitary gland from inflammatory, infectious, or hemorrhagic disorders. Secondary hypopituitarism is often bilateral, and hormone deficiency in this condition may be partial or complete. It may present as adrenal insufficiency, growth hormone, or gonadotropin deficiency. The clinical diagnosis, however, should include consideration of central hypothyroidism, adrenal insufficiency, and growth hormone deficiency as a unit, and each of these disorders should be actively sought. The initial diagnostic approach is to measure pituitary hormone levels, followed by radiologic imaging and clinical studies. If central hypothyroidism is present, it is likely that the deficiency is caused by impaired conversion of T4 to T3 (the main active form of thyroid hormone); a low T3 level is diagnostic of this disorder. When the diagnosis of hypopituitarism is confirmed, treatment is with appropriate doses of the deficient pituitary hormone(s).


The causes of hypothyroidism may be divided into two categories: primary and secondary. Primary thyroid failure is caused by destruction of the thyroid

text:
Hot Mess to Mindful Mom is a new book from Jada Shapiro, author of BrainChanger and Superhero Moms.
The author takes us through five levels of mindfulness: 

1.  The first step is learning to listen to your emotions rather than pushing them aside, or being overwhelmed by them.

2.  The second is learning to recognize that you have choices in any situation -- you can't change what has happened, or what is happening, but you can certainly change your reaction or attitude.

3.  The third is accepting your circumstances, your life, your body -- all the things you can't change.

4.  The fourth is getting comfortable with uncertainty, with never knowing what's going to come next.

5.  The fifth, and most advanced level, means being able to control your thoughts -- to let go of worries about the past or anxiety about the future, and to simply pay attention to what is right in front of you, in this very moment.

Mindful Mom offers suggestions and techniques for how to practice these steps, which Shapiro says will help you manage your day more effectively, reduce stress, and be the best parent you can be.

How does being mindful benefit everyone in your family? How do you practice

text:
Copyrighted works of writing, art, or music can’t be used for a commercial purpose without the copyright owner’s permission. Copyright law is an important part of every artist’s life. This information sheet is intended to help you understand the basics of copyright law and how it affects you and your career as a writer, artist, or musician.

What you should know

What is copyright?  Copyright is a form of protection that covers original works where authors and creators have a special right to control how the work is used. Works include books, music, movies, computer programs, photography, art, journals, blogs, websites, advertisements, etc.

Who owns the copyright?  The person who creates original works owns the copyright. Only if you write the story or music or create the picture do you own an automatic copyright. Copyright is protected under federal law.

What cannot be copyrighted?  Ideas, titles, concepts, facts, methods, slogans, systems, titles, and words cannot be copyrighted. They may, however, be protected by patent or trademark law.

How do I copyright my work?  You don’t have to file paperwork for a copyright unless you plan to sue someone. Registering a work of art, music or writing can be helpful, however.

Cesspool and sewer problems are dreaded by homeowners but unfortunately, not every homeowner is able to avoid these issues.

There are many different issues that can lead to a cesspool or sewer problem arising for the first time. This is something that no one likes to have to deal with because it can be a very messy and very costly solution to the issue. This is also why it is important to know how to prevent cesspool and sewer problems.

What Causes Cesspool and Sewer Problems?

There are more than one way that a cistern or septic tank can fail. These failures can lead to very costly problems and also a problem that can create some very unpleasant odor.

The main things that can cause a cesspit or tank to fail are roots that are growing into the tank. A failing cesspit also includes the fact that sludge build up can also cause the system to fail. The other issue is tree roots that can grow into the tank and cause it to fail and also a cracked pit or clogged pipes. Clogged up pipes also can be a problem because tree roots can enter the pipes and make them too clogged to function correctly.

In recent years, laser cutting has started to be used more widely. This advanced CNC process involves directing a powerful, high-energy beam of light at the stock material, heating it to a molten state, and breaking through while cutting cleanly and precisely. Compared to traditional milling or plasma cutting, laser cutting provides a more precise cut that is much easier to achieve. It is one of the most versatile processes available today, allowing metal fabricators to perform a wide range of cutting jobs on nearly any metal. With both water and air cooled equipment, a laser cutter can easily cut metals ranging from aluminum to stainless to bronze to copper. Even titanium and exotic alloys can be cut, making it a great option for manufacturers of aircraft or racing equipment.

water jet cutting process on large piece of metal

With Exchange Server 2013, there are certain changes in the way the data is stored in Exchange, making it difficult to recover data from a failed disk array, unless you’re willing to perform time-consuming data carving operations that might only extract parts of the mailbox.

To prevent this type of difficulty, Exchange 2013 introduced a new feature called Exchange Mailbox Redundancy. When this is enabled, it places a second copy of each mailbox database in a separate disk array. If Exchange is ever shut down, data replication is used to transfer the mailbox replicas to the new disk array.

text:
Why Many Businesses Choose to Incorporate in Nevada.
Businesses choose to incorporate in Nevada because of the state's favorable laws for companies, including the following:

  1. Nevada is a business friendly state
  2. Fewer corporate formalities in Nevada
  3. Nevada is highly protective of a corporation’s trade secrets and other confidential information
  4. Nevada law makes it clear that a corporation can enforce non-compete and non-solicitation agreements, and restricts those who have received notice of the agreement from discussing the details with others
  5. Corporations in Nevada enjoy a longer statute of limitation than other states
  6. Nevada corporations enjoy longer lasting protection for their trade dress than corporations in other states

For more information about the advantages of doing business in Nevada, please visit Business Advantage on our website.

Can Corporations Conduct Business in Other Countries?
Yes, corporate shares can be registered and/or traded on the foreign stock exchanges of countries such as Hong Kong, London, Frankfurt, Tokyo, Paris, and many others. However, corporate governance varies significantly from one jurisdiction to another, and in some cases may be completely different. Companies incorporated in Nevada can easily conduct business outside Nevada with a separate business structure that is governed by
prompt_prefix: Why Many Businesses Choose to Incorporate in Nevada.

Bitcoin is a currency, used to exchange goods and services, but its primary purpose is to serve as a medium of exchange. The supply of currency increases over time through coin creation, while the number of coins decreases over time thanks to the emission schedule. Because of the design of Bitcoin, it is expected to be deflationary, meaning that the amount of currency in circulation will rise in value over time.

The currency of Bitcoin is not controlled by any single institution, but rather by the community that uses it. The amount of currency available for exchange is determined by an open-source computer algorithm that runs on millions of computers and servers around the world. There are no government bailouts or back-door bailouts or even bailouts by any institution, because there is no central authority in charge.

In today’s digital age, there is no longer any need for an intermediary to process transactions. Transactions are conducted directly between people, using computers to make them. It is estimated that the total number of Bitcoins in circulation is now approximately $10 trillion.

Kendra DeFrain

You may have heard that Title IX is under attack, but you may not know what the law is or what it means to you.

Title IX was enacted in 1972. It has been in the news recently because the law has been under attack in recent years, most notably in Trump's budget proposal and Secretary of Education DeVos's attack on "equity." You may have heard that Title IX has something to do with sports, but you may not know what the law is or what it means for you.

Here is information to help you understand the law, your rights and how to get help. It is not legal advice. Please contact the BSMU Women's Center, Student Legal Services at (651) 295-3000, or the Office for Civil Rights at (800) 421-3481.

What is Title IX?

Title IX of the 1972 Education Amendments Act is a statute that prohibits discrimination on the basis of sex in education at all educational programs that receive any Federal funds. While there had been other discrimination laws on the books at the time, Title IX was the first federal law that said women could play an equal role in schools and

I would interpret both as meaning "I did not do well on the test." but the sentence with "very badly" means "I did even worse than I would normally do." As in, it was "unusually bad."

The other sentence just refers to the fact that I think I'm a pretty good student but I failed this test, therefore the test was bad (and this was a problem because the test was important.)

You can read it as "My performance on the test was poor." vs "I performed very poorly on the test." where the latter means more specifically and emphatically that you did especially, extraordinarily poorly on it.

The Daily Brief Newsletter • August 17th, 2015

By Alanna Ketler | Collective Evolution





How You Look Today Predicts How You’ll Age In The Future

According to their research, the researchers used genetic methods to track the biological age of a person. They looked at a group of 5,000 older people, some who were still in good health while others were living with a chronic health condition. This helped them to determine at what age different health declines started to occur.

They found that those who were experiencing healthy aging at the age of 70 didn’t actually look that much different than those people who were younger than 70 in terms of their facial features and how their skin looked.

According to scientists, they were able to predict with 92 percent confidence what a person’s biological age would be based on their photographs.

In past studies, scientists have been using a “facial structure method of aging,” based on the principle of how well people maintained the ratios between these structures, like the distance between the eyes and/or distance between eyebrows and jawline. This new approach was better at predicting how a person is aging, and the differences between two people.

Innovation from Outside-In, A View of 20200530-JD-FinB


This newsletter will look at the nature of innovation and how it is often viewed by organizations with some reflections on how this might fit within a ‘satisficing’ management perspective. 

‘Satisficing’ is a common term that has been around since at least the 1970s and refers to decision makers choosing an alternative that is good enough to avoid making the wrong choice. It has a long history going back to the 1930s. We see this often in our businesses. For example, organizations have to decide on which new product to invest in. Often a good enough decision is to look at new products that are similar to its current products. This is an efficient short path if a new product is to have a 15-year life cycle. This is often considered to be an easy decision – just buy another product with a few small changes. As a consequence, when organizations are innovating, many of the new products do not generate significantly different customer value. The market responds in a rational, often a slow, way, when organizations are not innovating in a new, more valuable, way.

Image courtesy of Maurice Benayoun.

The concept of Cyberearth emerged in the mid-90s. The concept was about how the new reality was defined by the digital and human interaction, and how it would change our relationship to nature. It would create a new understanding of nature in which we would experience the virtual as the natural part of our existence. It would change our understanding of nature, and it would lead us into a new kind of relationship with the planet. Cyberearth would be the new interface between humans and nature. The concept was based on the belief that the human species is going through a radical change, a change that requires a new way of thinking, a new way of interacting with nature. A new vision for the future of the relationship between humans and their environment.

Why you have “crying sessions and angry outbursts,” according to a psychiatrist.

The psychiatrist and psychoanalyst Dr. Judith Lewis Herman describes in Trauma and Recovery: The Aftermath of Violence – from Domestic Abuse to Political Terror how “during the period of acute post-traumatic stress, the traumatised subject will alternate between numbing and ‘crying sessions.’ The numbing state is dominated by a relentless, unthinking, obsessive repetition of the traumatic events or, in the absence of eyewitness experience, a ceaseless rumination of their implications. During the ‘crying sessions’ the individual is able to feel the full force of the traumatic experience. The numbing state alternates with periods of crying or rage, and sometimes with states of terror and anxiety. These ‘mood swings’ and ‘out-of-control feelings’ are commonly misinterpreted as ‘manic-depressive syndrome.’ At this stage, the patient is more like a ‘ticking time bomb’ than a manic-depressive, for the outbursts in rage or grief are not yet integrated into the patient’s experience of self. ‘Mood’ swings are likely to be triggered by external stimuli, especially a confrontation with one’s attacker.

The species is a diurnal butterfly which is on wing from late March to October. In spring, the butterflies often emerge after rain. The female lays about 500 eggs which are very tiny and are laid at distances of 0.5 m between each egg. The eggs hatch to black, red-striped larvae. The larvae feed on a variety of coastal broad-leaved trees and shrubs. The species may have more than one generation per year in southern populations, but in Britain no later than August. The chrysalis is green and marked with white. The pupal stage lasts two weeks. The adult butterfly has a wingspan of 5.7-6.8cm and is brown, the upper side with narrow, white eye-markings, and with small white spots on the forewings. The underside is green and mottled.

The larvae feed on a variety of broad-leaved trees and shrubs such as holly, broom, privet, and ivy.

The butterfly was originally described from the island of Minorca. The first adult was found there in 1838. The species has since become scarce

December by David Steffen Comments | Bookmark


BIO was formed to represent biotech’s voice in policy. That task is particularly important today as the field grows in criticality, continues to offer new solutions to some of our nation’s greatest challenges, and becomes more complex and multi-faceted. The science is complicated. BIO and its members will continue to work hard to keep the benefits of biotechnology solutions understood and accessible.

I know I'm in good company saying this, but the current debate over stem cell research and the NIH is a good example. In my opinion, the stem cell issue shows that the need for a biotechnology voice in policy is more important than ever.

In the stem cell debate, scientists can’t talk about biotechnology. It’s too complex. Scientists can talk about cloning. It’s much simpler. This debate will have real implications (see this) but we haven’t yet heard the biotech voice on it so far. The debate also illustrates how the general public and the press can’t understand how biotech research impacts them, except in the narrowest terms. That is, unless one can boil their message down to “cloning” or “genetic engineering.”

African Elephant's Ear


Botuglossaceae. Laminelliform monocot plants, with leaves borne in three ranks. Flowers in racemes or umbels or heads, with 3-3 mm long calyxes and 0.5-12 cm long corollas of 5 or 6 lobes, the middle lobe generally the largest. The outer parts of the tube of the corolla are thickened by a dense mass of woolly hairs (trichomes). Seeds enclosed in bony woody arils, which are attached to the inside of the fruit wall (perisperm).

Species 1: Pseudonaja africanus.
Distribution African mainland, and islands north of the tropical rainforest zone.

There is still no formal acceptance or description of Pseudonaja africanus as the African Elephant’s Ear. However, it is almost certainly the African Elephant’s Ear, and there are no better candidates, because the African Elephant’s Ear can only exist in Africa between the tropics of Cancer and Capricorn. The most likely candidate for the name Elephant’s Ear must be found in this region; also, Pernand-Rivière’s P. africanus fits exactly with C. B. Clarke’s Elephant

During rugby practice, Australian soldiers would often use the time to explain the game of rugby to their allied colleagues. Eventually, with the support of the Allied Expeditionary Force, which was based in France, and the Allied forces based in the French colony of New Caledonia, rugby was introduced to various units within the Allied Army.

### Single/mountain bus speed problems for R.Rahul

The distance between bus stops is 1.4km
It takes 5 seconds to accelerate from 0ms^{-1} to x and 15 seconds to decelerate from x to 0
So the total time taken to travel from one bus stop to the other is 5+5+15 = 25 seconds
Therefore the distance travelled by the bus in this time is (25)(25)km = 625m = 0.625km
Therefore, the speed when the driver presses the pedal (x) is x/(25) = (0.9)ms^{-1} = 0.18m/s = 72km/hr = 45.75mph
Hope this helps.

E. chelycetaceas

Tegua chelycetaceas

Chelycetidae are large snakes of Central and South America, often reaching lengths over 2 meters. The genus Erythrolamprus (formerly Atheris) was formerly considered to be a subgenus of Chelycetes, but is no longer recognized to be so.

Chelycetines have extremely large fangs and potent venom, but are not considered to be particularly dangerous – they tend to have fairly weak bites, as the fangs easily bend. They mostly prey upon frogs and lizards, and are diurnal in habit. The genus contains 14 recognized species:

* Atheris aesculapii Geoffroy 1803 of the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the Republic of the Congo, and the Central African Republic
* Atheris bilinearis Gray 1844 of Cameroon, Equatorial Guinea, Ghana, and possibly Gabon
* Atheris blomhoffii Gray 1844 of Liberia, Ivory Coast, and Ghana
* Atheris chlorechis Gray

Detailed instruction - why so bad (on Android OS at most)

The current manual

The manuals of almost all of the apps and programs that I use are filled with sentences like this:

The left window is used display the time of day. The right window shows you what time of day you set the alarm (see below).

This is not a single sentence, but there is no paragraph break, so I have to split it at the comma. It contains two instructions, but both are not really instructions, because both begin with 'you.'

The sentence is confusing because at first, it is not certain whether the time in the left window is for setting or showing. Later, when we get to the point, we are forced to stop reading and go back to understand. The fact that the right window shows you “what you set” is not an instruction but an explanation of a term. It is not necessary to have the first window show both dates, but that is the case to make the difference between the two possible uses of the left window more obvious. Why should your data backup data from physical locations to network appliances like Netstore rather than an off-site server?

A number of factors are involved, but the main advantage is the ease with which the data can be retrieved.

  • The data will always be accessible from your desk, as a network drive or, if your network is connected to the Internet, the data can be remotely accessed from anywhere that you have internet connectivity
  • The data can be managed by various policies, such as data retention and security
  • And the data can be centrally controlled.


How does Netmanage Netstore compare to other on-site data storage systems ?

The most obvious difference between a NetManage Netstore and an on-site server is the size of the Netstore.


  • The small floor-standing Netstore has a capacity up to 60GB.
  • The larger floor-standing Netstore has a capacity of up to 180GB.
  • The Netstore is designed to be unobtrusive in an office, rather than the more standard office servers which tend to be a bulky piece of heavy hardware.
  • Netmanage Netstore units can be wall or floor mounted.
  • The network is managed by Netmanage Netdrive which utilises Windows Explorer type functionality

You don't lose water you have the skin like me!

It sounds more of a statement than a question. Yet it makes a point and can easily be argued with. Yes, you do lose water. And yes, your skin will show the signs of it.

The human skin is mostly water, containing around 64 per cent of the total water within your body. Although you may be able to see the water in the glass before you drink it, you can only see the skin water by its reflection. But the water is there, and its importance to life and its maintenance is without question. You lose water all the time; from a number of sources which we shall explore in a moment.

Water Loss

From the moment you are born you start to lose the life supporting water from your body. The amount you lose depends upon your age, diet, environment and health. The greatest amount is lost during the most active phase of your life, from childhood to the menopause. Children, and in particular babies and toddlers, lose the most water per unit of body mass. They therefore need, as a rule of thumb, to replace 14 to 16 ounces for every pound of their body weight daily. They lose water from their bodies in several different ways.

By admin Published: Augual,17 , 13:45

The US Women's Sports Foundation, one of the most respected and well-known sports organizations in the US, is a non-profit corporation that is committed to advancing the well-being and opportunities for girls and women through participation in organized sports. The organization was founded in 1999 by 13 prominent members of industry, philanthropy and sport to promote girls' and women's sports.

In its 10-year history, the USWSF has been successful in reaching its goal of promoting the advancement of girls and women in sports by advocating for legislation that promotes Title IX compliance, supporting the growth of female leadership in sports and providing millions of dollars in funding to support the development and growth of girls' sports organizations.

Why is Title IX needed?

Title IX is a civil rights law that, among many other things, requires schools that participate in federally funded athletic programs to give both men’s and women’s athletes “substantial, equitable and nondiscriminatory athletics opportunities."

What do I need to find out?

Many schools may have a team of male and female athletes, but they’re not complying with the “substantial, equitable and nondiscriminatory athletics opportunities" for women that Title IX mandates.

The Pessimization Effect




A major flaw with research which focuses only at extreme situations. And in extreme situations we can expect a different outcome to happen: what the researchers call “survivorship bias”, where they sample a non-representative group of the population. But then there are lots of other types of bias which come along, which I don’t think they discuss.

For example, I’d be highly interested to see the researchers study which people they asked their questions to: those who had recently experienced a big event or change (a promotion, for example) may well have a different take then those who are generally the most optimistic, and those people may be more susceptible to the Pessimization Effect.

Another bias which I’d be highly interested to see the researchers study is confirmation bias. People often look for evidence which confirms what they believe while ignoring any evidence which contradicts them.

Finally, and most importantly, I’d like to see the researchers address why the pessimization effect exists at all. If the participants in the second experiment knew that they would feel more pessimistic after reading the article, why did they read it in the first place?

In February 1992, Argentina's economy fell into free-fall. The country's president, Fernando de la Rua, panicked and quickly sought the help of Argentina's central bank. After all, it was de la Rua's government that had given hundreds of millions of dollars in tax breaks to companies he did business with. Now, in the midst of a national emergency, the banks were being asked to contribute to society, and the bank's president was refusing?

It is a state constitutional duty of each state in this Union to care for its delinquent or criminal female population. To a considerable extent, this has been done by each state. Women prisoners are an extremely hard to handle class. Even the most ordinary, non-delinquent woman, is not easy for the average male, or even his female associate, to cope with on a daily basis. The same holds true for the prisoner.

The average male will probably never know what it is like to live with 40 women. It is extremely difficult to control them and to keep them together and productive prisoners.

What was so horrible about the Great Fire's aftermath

by: Mollie, Elizabeth & Katie 

What did the fire start from?

The origin of the fire was the bakery of Thomas Farriner at Pudding Lane in the early hours of 2 September 1666. This was not far from the houses of other bakers in the area. A maid had left some flammable scraps around the fire, which spread to the adjacent building in the bakery. Another eyewitness, the Lord Mayor of London Sir Thomas Bloodworth, claimed that there was only a minor blaze: "A great fire has happened at the Mansion House. I order you to pull it down." The Lord Mayor failed to give the fire any urgent attention, instead ordering his sheriffs to demolish adjoining houses to make a firebreak. Bloodworth later stated that the Lord Lieutenant, Sir Maurice Wemyss, ordered him to prevent people from crossing London Bridge. When Bloodworth reached Pudding Lane he "was informed that above 100 houses had been burned".

What kind of effect did this have on the city?

The fire destroyed about 15,000 buildings, leaving 70,000 homeless. The total area and mortality can be determined with some certainty only within the city wall.

　An m-mingol is represented with four integers and four lowercase a,b,c,d characters. If we label the integers as a, b, c and d, an m-mingol is denoted by a - b - c - d.For example, 12 - 6 - 8 - 5 is an 3-mingsol. The integers a, b, c and d satisfy (1 <= a <= b <= c <= d <= 10000) (a % b ~=~ 0) (a % c ~=~ 0) (a % d ~=~ 0)In this problem, we ask you to output the minimum number of 2-mingsols formed by the given 2-mingsols. It's guaranteed that the answer can be uniquely represented by 3 decimal digits.

Input Sample
There is only one case. The first line contains one integer T (1 <= T <= 5) denoting the number of cases. Each case contains one integer N, denoting the number of given 2-mingsols in that case. 2. You are looking straight ahead, so you are facing north west, therefore you are at a 45 degree angle. We can describe this as 45N45W.
3. You turn 30 degrees clockwise, you are now at a 15 degree angle. 15N45W in degrees.
4. Your next leg is a straight line, straight east. Which makes 30N90W.
5. You turn 40 degrees clockwise to north east. At a 55 degree angle. 55N65W in degrees.
6. Turn left 60 degrees. Now facing north by north east. (1N65W)
7. Turn 65 degrees clockwise. Now facing north west. (1N50W)
8. Travel straight west

Hoi An Ancient City Hoi An Hoi An Cham Culture History

Cham Clothing

The Cham wore very colorful skirts like the Kinh and Muong (Viet). These were usually shorter than the Vietnamese, but were often made of very fine, expensive silks for the upper classes. The upper class wore blouses with a front slit and a long tail, while the female underclass only wore long skirts. Some Chams wore long tunics, others wore only short breeks with sashes or belts. The Vietnamese often remarked their preference for the Cham male dress, as it was more decorative than the Vietnamese male dress. The Cham wore silver jewellery, particularly earrings and nose rings. Women also wore gold bangles around their waist and wore gold necklaces with large gold earrings. They made their own gold pendants and gold bracelets.

The Cham people have always had a reputation for their rich brocades, with their rich golden thread on a deep, dark rich green which they are famed for in present-day Vietnam. They used these beautiful materials for their own clothes, as well as the court dress of the kings and their queens. They were also

The ship-owner in Sönerske Viken employed around 5 000 persons. For each vessel, there were at least six ships' mates on board for safety, in addition to engineers and a captain, so that in general, each ship required about 15-20 crew or more. In total, this resulted in 80-100 men who sailed with The Sönerske Line all year round. There were also some sailors who were employed continuously. Other crews were needed to assist in loading the ships. Around 60 men were employed in the steamships, a slightly smaller number in sailing ships.

The ship-owner had a number of crew and employees on hand, who were employed at the quayside, and various jobs were allocated to different employees according to their skills.

The energy storage race is on - who will deliver?




With the ever mounting urgency and pressure to find solutions to the growing and overwhelming challenges we face from a changing climate, it is important to ask the question that I first stumbled upon several years ago when investigating the impact of energy storage on the grid - do we have a ‘best technology’ that will enable us to store energy in vast quantities in order for us to meet our future energy demands in a low-carbon and sustainable way? Or can we learn to work with the energy we have already in a more effective and efficient way?


What is the challenge?


When it comes to harnessing renewable energy sources, like wind and solar, two of the main challenges are intermittency and scale. It is an age-old practice to be self-sufficient at a local level, whether by growing your own food, generating your own electricity and managing your waste. If everyone had space to generate their own energy at the same level of energy consumption, this would be fine. But when it comes to providing energy not just to each individual home, but on a much greater scale through the energy grid, you need consistent and sustainable access to energy at all times.

SDB-SHIF (Standard Distance Beam Spot Search)

If desired a further processing of the data is possible. Here, the distances and heights to the objects are analyzed in order to determine which direction and distance will provide the best illumination. This is particularly relevant where long distances or large areas must be illuminated. Furthermore, it identifies the area which is exposed to excessive amounts of light; this is necessary to optimize illumination. In addition, with SDB-SHIF calculations it is possible to detect areas which require additional or reduced illumination and thus avoid areas where the product to be treated is exposed to excessive amounts of light.

The calculations are performed using standard distances of 100, 200 and 500 mm and beam angles of 5°, 20° and 60°. With these values the illumination is compared with the minimum and maximum irradiation values. A calculation in the SHIF Mode is recommended when an area must be illuminated with a large distance between the light source and product being treated.

The SDB-SHIF mode can also identify the areas with high excess light levels. Here, it is necessary to adjust the distance between the light source and the product being processed. If this adjustment is not possible, an additional screen can be introduced to decrease the intensity

Generally, an installation program will create several keys in the windows registry. For example, for a word processor, a key might be created that looks like this (the brackets denote optional data):

   [HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Borland word proccessor\version_1\program]

Other keys might also be created, such as a key to store the installation data in for later use by the help system. Another key might be created to store the path to the file (this file would contain all of the different file associations as well as the location of various help and program files such as icons, fonts, macros, etc.) There might also be keys created for user level data. This is data that you customize yourself, such as creating your own file

How do birds survive at a young and vulnerable age with just its nest that has no proper shelter?


How do birds survive at a young and vulnerable age with just its nest that has no proper shelter from natural calamities and predators? How birds develop the ability of flight when they don't have any parent to show them how to fly?


Short answer: by keeping their eggs warm! And by instinct.

Birds do not develop flight, they are born able to fly, just in the same way that a newborn baby has instincts developed over millions of years (e.g. fear of snakes) in order to survive. Birds just have more developed instincts than most other animals because they have to take care of their young without much help from the adults.

They have two eggs because when one dies or is removed for any reason they only need one chick to survive and that doesn't need as much food as two. Some birds have multiple eggs by chance (e.g. because it hasn't found a mate yet). For example, robins can have two nestlings but only lay one egg at a time which means a robin has never been lucky enough to have three nestlings, and will not lay two eggs at a time because it is safer for the mother to only have one egg

Some Basic Guidelines To Good Photography...

© 2018 Robert M. Lindsey Photography

There's really no secret to good photography. All it takes is attention to detail. Let's start with the main guidelines...

1. Exposure. The most critical aspect of photography is proper exposure because if the exposure is not right, your photo will appear too dark, too light, or will have over or under exposures in parts of the photographic image. If you do not have full manual control of your camera, then you should definitely use the exposure settings to get a good look at the scene your going to photograph prior to taking your photo. This takes practice, so do a lot of test shots until you have it down. This is something that can be improved upon, so please see my exposure technique page for more information.

2. Focus. Be sure your photographic subject is in focus and not blurry. The camera focus point is very sensitive during the exposure, so it takes practice to properly focus. Some cameras let you focus on a separate spot away from the focusing point.

Detailed Description This proposal changes rule so entities receive income when they own assets of debt issuer not for the purpose of obtaining money, but rather to obtain a security for a third party. This is an expansion of the policy. For example, under the current definition, if a third party buys debt of an entity in a sale and leaseback structure, the entity is not receiving income since it is not for its own use. However, this third party would receive a security interest, and income would otherwise be received. This is an expansion of the policy since the current rule recognizes a security received by an entity for use by another entity (e.g., for use by a controlled entity). While these are both instances when security are involved, our policy differentiates where the security is received and for whom. Under the expanded definition, income is recognized when the control is transferred from the seller to the buyer, irrespective of the intention of the transferee. This does not change the timing of the income, only the entity that is to recognize. This change provides more consistency with FASB Codification Section 400-10-31-1 (a), which defines the time of control received. The new language is not broader than the current sentence under the phrase "and its subsidiaries"

Astrazelas – Sins to Rebound upon

In Atoning – Explanatory Notes and Comments (see page 14) it is pointed out that “In the Greek Bible the word usually rendered “sin” is astráthē. The literal meaning of it is “a throwing down, fall.” Hence, the word means a trespass, a sin or error. Astrátha means a casting down or hurling down, a committing to destruction or ruin”

It is true that Astráthē has that primary literal meaning, and is often used that way in the Scriptures. But the meaning “error” is not a legitimate meaning of Astráthē at all. The Greek for error is “áthēma” (hence “atheism”) and the meaning Astráthē in Scripture is not that, but means “error” only in the figurative or derived sense.

The primary concept is that of a throwing down, a casting, as upon a rock, and hence “fall.” It is in that concept that a deeper understanding may be found in our verse, for the deeper meaning is that of a throwing down to a particular place, a destination or result, and so “throwing down upon the rock.” Now

Hornbierkamp's Diner-Restoran-Visi (Sermsa) (formerly: Diner-Restoran Visi)

Hornbierkamp's Diner-Restoran-Visi (Sermsa) (formerly: Diner-Restoran Visi) (Kampong Gajah, Visi, East Java) is a restaurant in Indonesia.
Building.
Hornbierkamp's was built between 1912 and 1918 and has a Dutch colonial style. It is made of teak timber from East Java. The restaurant has two long rooms which are located in different yards, but a hallway connects them.
There was little furniture in the restaurant during the colonial era. There were only two tables and benches on the ground floor, one table and two stools on the first floor, and one table on the terrace. The first dining room and terrace were used by Europeans. The first floor was occupied by native Indonesians who sat on the ground, the so-called "floor-sitters".
The restaurant is also a museum. Many utensils used in the restaurant before 1970, such as large bowls, plates, spoons, knives, and glassware are displayed.

The purpose behind your goal is a key ingredient in the process of goal definition. You will need to answer two questions to determine the purpose behind your goal. If you already have your goals, we will review how to refine them with clarity. Even if you have well-defined goals that are working with certainty, we will explore how to get more from the process with even more clarity and certainty.

Answering these two questions will help you identify the purpose behind your goal.

  1. Why do you want this goal?
  2. What will you get from accomplishing your goal?

Problem with R: multiple variables for lmFit function?



Let there two variable to explain. Can I use lmFit function from the package RDPLOT in this case, when the explanatory variables have different name?



Yes, you can do this by assigning different values to the var.labels parameter of the lmFit function from the RDPLOT package:

> library(RDPLOT) 
> data("HSA") 
> p <- lmFit(HSA, var.labels = c("x", "y"))
> plot(p)

enter image description here


To be more explicit about what's going on here, it would be useful to see what the p object is comprised of:

> p
lmfit(formula = log2p ~ x + y, data = HSA, var.labels = x y)

Call:
lmfit(formula = log2p ~ x + y, data = HSA, var.labels = x y)

America cannot maintain the level of security required to preserve her liberty and freedom without the aid of other countries. This is a simple statement, but with far reaching and profound ramifications to the future of America and her empire.

Student A decided that if John had bought five packs at Costco on Saturday, then Sarah, on Sunday, could buy them for her 6 friends. To do this, Sarah would buy 6 packs.

Student B nodded in agreement. "That makes sense. So, Sarah is buying 6 packs, and we know that three packs for 1 person costs $15. How do we calculate the price per pack?"

Student A thought for a moment. "If 3 packs cost $15, then 1 pack costs $15 divided by 3, which is $5."

Student B smiled. "Right, so if 1 pack costs $5, and Sarah is buying 6 packs, what would be the total cost?"

Student A quickly calculated. "If each pack costs $5, then 6 packs would cost 6 times $5, which is $30."

Student B shook her head. "Wait, that's not right. We need to remember that the price of 3 packs is $15, not $5. So, if Sarah is buying 6 packs, we should calculate it as 6 times $5, which is $30 divided by 2, because 6 packs is twice as much as 3 packs."

 What is a google account?

A google account (aka a google profile) is your online identity in Google services. It allows you to keep your preferences and customize your experience with our services. You can control which services you connect with your profile and decide the information shared across them.

How can you access your account?

You can access your Google Account from a computer browser using the URL  You can also use your Android, iOS, or other cell phones by using the Google app.

Your google account contains information about:

  • Your name
  • Your picture
  • Alternate e-mail addresses
  • Other Google profiles you created
  • Billing and update settings
Your google account is your identity everywhere across Google.

First, the nature of how caves develop.

Caves develop in four ways:

  1. Erosion of bedrock.
  2. Seepage of water through the sides of mountains.
  3. Subterranean erosion through underground rivers. Note: "underground rivers" doesn't refer to a single flowing body of water. Instead, it refers to a number of smaller underground channels, generally below the water-table level.

Can Companies Reduce Operating Expense by Selling Carbon Quoters in the Emission Trading System?

Companies that produce greenhouse gases have to hold carbon quotas in the Emissions Trading System, as required by law, and can sell them to other companies that need to meet the required quota.
The price for such carbon quotas depends on the level of demand and supply. It is determined on a free market, but the price is set by brokers as the price in that trade is not yet known.

Companies who can reduce emissions can generate additional revenue by producing less carbon and selling the additional amounts that they do not need as carbon quotas.

For carbon neutral companies (zero-carbon neutral) with a surplus of carbon quotas, the options for selling them are an important source of income.

In this case, the amount of unused carbon quotas sold at the time is the primary determinant of the actual amount of revenue that can be earned.

What to Ask People?



How To Find Answers: When Do We Find or Make Answers?

The question is how do we find answers in situations where we don’t understand? Answers that are beyond the scope of our knowledge and experience? In other words, what do we do when faced with a situation that we cannot answer with knowledge we already understand by ourselves? Or do we?

Do we rely on some external reference (such as information from others) or do we ‘make it up’? In other words, do we use knowledge from someone else to solve our problems, or do we develop the knowledge or information that we need?

We usually make one of two choices, one from information we already know or the other from information learned (or developed) by others:

  1. We find other’s information, use it in our minds, combine it with other information, evaluate it, analyze it, and develop an answer.
  2. Or, we think we already know enough and come up with an answer that fits our assumptions and mental models of what we expect.

If we make ‘up’ answers, how do we know if the answers we think we are developing are really correct? I will look at that in the next blog!

Tiger cactus was the name of a music venue in Toronto’s East End in the 1960s. It was the site of the psychedelic jam sessions featuring the Holy Modal Rounders and The Fugs, in particular. The venue was also owned by the Cabbagetown hippies themselves. (More info here.)

The Tiger cactus is also a specific genus of cactus (Stenocereus) also known as the Christmas cactus. These cacti are native to the West Indies and South America. They were also called “jumping cactus” because their fruit would jump off the plant.

The cactus is thought to have been brought to the United Kingdom during the 18th century.  The plants spread rapidly and they are now one of the most common ornamental cacti in British North America.

What can cause dizziness in children? What’s dizziness? Dizziness is described by a loss of balance or sensation of whirling. Vertigo is the feeling that there is a motion in the environment when there is nothing at all. The cause of these sensations can be a multitude of things. A headache could indicate a blood clot, which is the more severe potential cause.

In the case of a child with ADHD, dizziness can also be caused by a condition similar to vertigo, a symptom of ADHD. Vertigo can be caused by excess pressure on the inner ear, which is caused by fluid drainage into the ear from the outer ear by the ear drum.

While these may seem unrelated, dizziness could be caused by the same things that cause vertigo. Excess pressure on the inner ear from the fluid draining in the ear can cause loss of balance and loss of appetite. While we wouldn’t have thought it, excess pressure on the inner ear could also be caused by ear infections.

The question mentions a Piper plane flying alongside a Cessna from the start. We need to establish the position of the airplanes based on the given scenario. 

Initially, both airplanes are parallel to each other and flying at their respective constant speeds. Given that the Piper plane flies 50 mph faster than the Cessna on the ground, it has a 100-mile head start. Since their ground speeds are different, we should consider these separately. Let's denote the speed of the Cessna as \(v_c\) mph and the speed of the Piper (ground) as \(v_p\) mph where \(v_p = v_c + 50\).

Since they both travel 50 miles to catch up to each other, we can set up the following equations based on the distances covered by each plane:

User Story or Function Name – Should A Test Get Tested?

| Comments () | Technologies

One of my colleagues has been working on a new web app recently. He had some stories ready to get the system tested and he asked me whether we should write the stories as the name of a function name.

This is something I've seen (or probably done) in the past, and I've always thought that the name of a function and a story are in fact two very different things.

A story represents a user and how they would interact with the system. The user will have a goal or intention in interacting with the system, for which a function may or may not exist. The story describes this user goal, while the function represents how the system fulfills this goal. (I have recently heard the term “feature” used instead of “function” in the context of web pages, but I think the analogy holds true in the context of a web application.)

I like thinking of it as the difference between a use case and a class diagram. The use case describes the interaction between the system and the user, whereas the class diagram represents functions that fulfil a use case. In this analogy, the role of the programmer is the class diagram, and the role of the tester is

The SRY Gene on the Y Chromosome

The Y chromosome in the human male contains the sex determining gene, called the SRY gene. This gene codes for a protein that regulates the activity of many other genes. SRY stands for sex-determining region Y-gene. This gene lies in the middle part of the Y chromosome and is transmitted to the offspring by the father.

The SRY gene has a precise sequence of base pairs. SRY is a normal gene in males, but, in females, it has a mutation or genetic defect, which causes it to be nonfunctional.

The SRY gene is responsible for initiating sex differentiation.

Spain or officially the Kingdom of Spain is the leading country in north western Spain. The Muslim empire was over-run at the end of the middle ages with the union and expansion of the Christian European empires . The last of the Moor rulers , the Muslim Emirate of Granada, was defeated in 1492 by the Catholic Monarchs . Köp report

The concept of sustainable fashion was first introduced in the 1980s. In the beginning the focus was on ecological aspects and the fashion industry’s material and environmental impact. When it became clear that the problem was more than merely ecological and it could not be solved without considering the production processes. The next step was to include social responsibility and fair trade.

However, as fashion consumers are also concerned with environmental and social aspects, it is important for the industry to continue to emphasise those issues. In order to do so, it is important to look at the concept from different angels and not only concentrate on certain aspects.

To gain a better insight into fashion consumers’ values, attitudes and actions, it is important to look at the concept of sustainable fashion from various perspectives. In order to provide new and relevant answers on how to achieve a more sustainable fashion future, this report is therefore divided into four parts. In each section, an academic article is combined with input material from companies, associations and other organisations that work in the fashion industry. The aim is to provide the reader with an overview of the present situation

What is a Chord?

The term chord, as applied to music, refers to three or more notes played simultaneously. Generally, this refers to three or more instruments playing together, but there are also cases in which multiple notes are played at the same time by one instrument. Chords are essential to any form of western music whether it be pop, jazz, classical, rock, country, blues or a million other variations in between. The more notes and the more complex the chord, the more interesting the sound becomes. Chords add definition to a song's melody by providing accompaniment for it.

Why do singers sing along to the bass?

You can use any instrument to make a chord, but it's most common to use the bass and guitar or the piano. Most musicians understand the bass as the low tones that ground any music. But the bass guitar also makes chords; it sounds like a guitar or piano chord with less volume and it is more of a low end sound than piano or guitar chords. It is most often used to ground the song and harmonize the chord progress

Museum Review of Drunk with Beer in China for January.

If history serves us at all, it is this: Chinese beer is no way, shape, or form similar to any type of beer we have in America. The name may be the same, but the ingredients, manufacturing process, and taste are much different. While Chinese beer is in a general state of poor taste compared to most American beers (although this is not a complete judgment call as not all American beers are created equal, and some are very good) the Chinese have their own special way of producing beer that is uniquely theirs. However, because this unique process of brewing is rather new, and due to rapid industrial advances in China in the 1980’s, there is relatively very few historical records of the ancient Chinese brewing tradition. There is very rarely mention of any kind of large-scale brewing in Chinese history, and it’s been said that the earliest mention of brewing appears in some of the earliest Buddhist texts.

But the one Chinese beer that has had the strongest impact and influence on American beer is Tsingtao Beer.  Established in Qingdao, Shandong Province in 1903, Tsingtao is the largest Chinese brewing company, and one of the largest breweries in the world

It is based on three measures: life expectancy, education and per-capita gross domestic product adjusted for relative cost of living. In 2010, the Human Development Index was calculated for countries as diverse as Afghanistan and the United States.

There are already some happiness indexes. The Happy Planet Index, for example, takes into account some factors other than economic ones. Practical tips

Here are some pointers for getting up and running quickly and painlessly:

  • Get a simple and powerful text editor
  • Get familiar with basic unix commands - you probably don't need more than this for your data analysis.
  • A note on reproducibility: There are certain tools that work best with specific file formats. That is, if I run a data processing pipeline and get nice results, but I can't recreate it for you, the only solution might be for you to recreate the process on your own. If I give you a nice shiny executable pipeline, which you can't reproduce because I haven't told you what it is I did, well, that's worse than useless. You also don't want to have lots of scripts lying around, because it's difficult for someone to figure out which are relevant and what is outdated.

What Are the Effects of a Stroke. Stroke affects the entire body, from the mind and the heart to the ability to walk and talk, and the effects of a stroke can affect different parts of the body differently. Brain damage occurs quickly. The effects of a stroke depend on which areas of the brain are damaged.

Do You Have a Stroke?

The best way to store medicine is in a sealed container in a cool, closeted place (65 to 77 degrees F with a relative humidity of 75 percent or lower), away from sunlight, heat and direct contact with other medicines, says the Food and Drug Administration.

The FDA also advises, “Avoid storing medicines in the bathroom. The temperature and humidity in the bathroom can be significantly higher than other areas of the home.”

When you're done using the medication, the FDA wants you to “throw away the leftover medicine,” except for medicines that your healthcare provider has approved or your pharmacist has said it is OK to “save.”

Follow those regulations, and you'll be doing what's required by law, says the FDA. To make sure your medicine has a longer shelf life, you're supposed to “store it as directed by your healthcare provider, pharmacist or the manufacturer.”

November:12 — What has been regarded as the main cause of cancer has been transformed into an effective way to fight cancer.

By combining drugs with iron, a novel technique kills cancer cells while leaving healthy cells unharmed. The study was published in the online version of Nature Chemistry on 16 Sept.

This study, led by Dr. Xiaohua Wu, a researcher of the Shanghai Medical College of Fudan University, shows that under magnetic force, the chemo drugs can be targeted to cancer cells. When they enter cancer cells, iron ions can bind to hydroxyl radicals to form a stable compound, which further triggers cancer cell apoptosis.

Iron can not only enter cells naturally through iron channels, but also be transported into cells by magnetic force.

“We first introduced drugs to cancer cells through a magnetic field, and then introduced different forms of iron to bind to the chemo drugs in the cancer cells to make iron compound. The iron compound has been known to kill cancer cells,” Wu said.

The Fokker F28 Fellowship is a regional jet airliner built by Fokker between 1965 and 1983. It was the last major design of Fokker’s founder Anthony Fokker, who supervised the program before his death in 1972. The first flight of the first prototype was made in the summer of 1966 and the type received certification in 1971. The F28 was Fokker’s most successful design, with 225 aircraft produced during its twelve years of production, and was marketed throughout Europe, the Middle East, and South East Asia.

The design of the F28 was very modern for the time it was introduced. The jet was designed primarily to meet the needs of regional airlines with aircraft of 50 to 75 passenger capacity, filling the gap between then current turboprops, primarily the Vickers VC10 and British Aerospace 146, and large jets like the Sud Aviation Caravelle, Douglas DC-9 and Boeing 737. By the 1970s the market for regional airplanes shifted to higher capacity regional jets (50

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia. (Redirected from Rondanya)

Institute of Art of Arqueiro [Institut del arte d'Arqueiro in Catalan] or Castellers or Arqueadors is a sport invented in the early 19th century. It was created and developed in the Rondanya district in the municipality of Valls, in the Alt Camp region of Catalonia, Spain. Originally, the purpose was to build the human towers as tall as possible. Nowadays, it is a traditional and cultural event. The main objective of the Castellers is building the Human Towers. They are built in four sections known as Pinya, Tronc, Càrrec and Bases. The castellers, usually from the same neighbourhood, climb from the ground to the top of the Human Tower supported by those below.

The Pinya (Base) is a large group of people that keep the Castell solid. Their task is to protect both the people who fall from the tower and the Tower itself. They are also the ones who raise the Tronc.

Infinite monkey principle


For all purposes that this can answer any better or otherwise you can get them at the original site - http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Infinite_monkey_theorem. It only has a small amount of mathematical content in it and the rest is just references but they are also to other sites.

The best way to do it is probably to work out how many possibilities there are - i.e. all the letters in all the possible positions.

So you have 26 letters and 26 possible places they can be at - so $26^{N}$ (where N is the number of places). Once you need to know the probability (or frequency) that your message will fit in those places.

So the probability that those $N$ positions are all filled with English letters is $\frac{\text{number of words of that size}}{\text{total permutations of N letters in 26 different letters}}$ Now, it depends if the message is the same from one set of letters as another - if it is then you just need to add them all up.

So say we just need a sequence of 26 letters, the probability would be $\frac{1}{26^{26}}$ (a billion billion times less than 1).

Dear Andrea,

I'm not sure if I understand your question.  So, please correct me if I'm wrong. 

When a Libertarian Party member nominates you for the office of Clerk of Court, that's a nomination.  In some states it's called a petition.   You have to collect and submit signatures by a certain deadline to that State's Secretary of State to be placed on the ballot.  The Secretary of State, in turn, will certify that you are on the ballot.

To answer your question, in most states you need to collect a certain number of signatures to qualify for the ballot.  For example, in Wisconsin the requirements are:

A candidate for county office shall submit 150 signatures of registered electors, 100 signatures of electors residing in the city or village where he intends to file to run. However, where a primary or general election is otherwise affected, the candidate shall submit 450 signatures.

What mechanism can somebody take where by we get access to the data stored in encrypted form (and which can't be deciphered by any one else)? So the question is that what steps they can take to decrypt it. What mechanism will be used by them so that they don't have to break the encryption (because it is impossible)?


It depends on the encryption method used for the e-mail. If it is public-key encryption then each user must either exchange his/her key with the recipient in person or rely some kind of key exchange system. The best kind of key exchange is to generate a strong password, exchange it in person and use it to decrypt the e-mail. The next best thing is to use an encryption keys service like GnuPG (http://www.gnupg.org/ ) or PGP (http://www.pgp.com/ ) where the receiver has already installed an encryption key pair on the computer he/she is working on and uses it to decrypt the message.

This being said, if you are the one who has the key pair then you will be able to decode every message that has been encoded with your private key. For that to happen, the sender must have your public key and have used it

A lot about running and recovery for beginners.

The first chapter that really stood out to me was about why so many injuries happen in the beginning stages of training. The most important one was the idea of over-training while you are a beginner. How do you fix that? I would have never given this idea a second thought, but I have run into plenty of runners that don’t understand that it’s okay to skip a run, take a rest day, or take a couple of weeks off and not worry about your mileage or how fast you are running. 

This leads into Chapter 2 which was about finding balance. This was about when an athlete goes from the couch to the pavement, they’re not ready to push through fatigue and soreness. This may not stop anyone, but the body is still learning how to deal with that sort of stress. In order to get the body stronger, you need to have balance and strength in other areas first, not just running. You need to know when it’s time to scale back and rest.

Is the background colour you’ve written CSS3 animations over different from different versions?

No, and this is probably a misconception, due to one particular example.

When writing CSS backgrounds using multiple gradient colors instead of specifying opacity on a color gradient, the color background behind the animation might differ from the same view in another browser, due to that browser calculating the opacity of the element on it’s own differently. This can sometimes result in the background showing more “through” the animation than it should. You can prevent this by defining the opacity of the background and/or the element with the background, or, just write all your CSS animations with 100% opacity.
This is not, however, limited to browsers that support CSS3 animations. The difference of opacity between CSS gradient backgrounds occurs in almost all major browsers. Also, you should not forget to consider the case where the background image of the web page is not a solid color, but is transparent in parts, as this too, might result in the color background of the animation being different.

Blog Subject: The Evolution of Human Sexuality
Tuesday, 27 April 2004

Evolution of Human Sexuality

In The Evolution of Human Sexuality, Donald Symons (1979) argues that we cannot understand human sexuality unless we understand the way natural selection shaped it. To do this, we must study the mating behavior of other species to learn what natural selection does and then compare it to the ways we are similar to these species; and to study what happens in populations when people lose their natural habitat by using modern medicine and technology to control it and study the effects that this loss has on sexual reproduction. This gives us a picture of what we would be like without modern medicine. When we study the way natural selection shaped human sexuality we must keep in mind that, for most of its evolution, homo sapiens had lived mainly as nomadic hunters and gatherers.

The Science of Hiking

Most of us understand that physical exertion produces heat, and that evaporation of sweat removes heat from the body - and that means the sweatier we are, the more cooling we are doing. A 1999 study by the American Council on Exercise found that the cooling rate due to evaporation of sweat is four times better than the cooling of the skin by wind. But how many of us fully understand just how important this heat loss is when it comes to keeping the body running at a temperature where the cells can survive?

It may surprise you to know that the human body is a highly engineered system that has its own highly effective air conditioning system. When it's hot and we exercise, our body automatically switches on the coolant system. The coolant is sweat, released by glands under the skin, and the coolant's effect is increased by evaporation from the skin to the surrounding air, which is where we cool down by as much as 70 per cent. But we need the surrounding air to be able to absorb this sweat through evaporation

Biomethane: a clean source of energy

Biomethane is an increasingly well-known gas with a range of different uses. Here are some of its advantages in our daily lives...


Biomethane is becoming more and more used as a gas for heating and energy. Since its commercial launch in 2010, 5 600 French people consume it daily.

Where does it come from?

Biomethane consists of extracting the methane contained in biodegradable waste. This can be done in one of two ways. The first type of extraction comes from organic matter that has been biodegraded in landfills and from which the gas is extracted. The second comes from organic material undergoing anaerobic digestion. This organic material needs to be collected and transformed into digestate prior to biomethane extraction.


Why produce it?

Using biomethane is good for the environment because it does not create carbon dioxide emissions. When we burn gas, it releases water, methane and carbon dioxide. This Week Health
The Most Efficient, Effective AI Health

It seems all of sudden there is a new AI health product for every condition. A lot of what we see, unfortunately, is not ready for prime time, even though people are still getting funded through the traditional venture capital route. We just don't think it should be this way. 

It’s time we did what we can to make AI in healthcare more efficient and effective.

There are many products out there with lots of noise that are not backed by the facts.

The health ecosystem is filled with hype and promise of artificial intelligence products that might have limited effectiveness or efficiency in the real world. While some may claim the technology has arrived, that’s not always the case.

It seems all of sudden there are new AI health products for every medical condition. We have seen lots of them come and go, and they were not ready to make a difference for a doctor or administrator. A lot of what we see is not ready for prime time, even though people are still getting funded through the traditional venture capital route. We wish it was different because it seems the money is everywhere, and yet we do not have a cure for cancer. It makes us rethink how innovation in technology gets the same type of investment.
